exactText: שֶׁ קָדֵ SanctificationQadesh Fill the glass good and full of wine, and with the qiddush, laud the Lord divine. Émpi il bicchiero ben pieno di vino e di chidusc laude al Signor divino. ְיֵינוֹ דִי וִינ פִֶּיִי אִיל בִּיקְיֵרוֹ בֵּין פֶּ אֵימְ לַאוּדֵי שֶׁוּדֵּ אֵי דִי קִ אַל סִינְיוֹר דִיוִינוֹ׃ From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 We will be reciting an extended version of the Qiddush. Although there are instructions printed between, one should not pause between the portions. Thus we will read the instructions first. To begin, the blessing over wine – Yayin. When the seder falls on Friday night,, the following passage is added. ץ מַ֥יִם וְהָאָ֖ר שָָּׁ י׃ וַיְכֻלּ֛וּ הַ שִָּֽׁ שִָּׁ ר י֥וֹם הַ יְהִי־בֹ֖ק ֽ ב וַ ֥ר יְהִי־ע ֽ וַ ֣ר שֶׁ בִיעִ֔י מְלַאכְתּ֖וֹ אֲ שְָּׁ יּ֣וֹם הַ בַּ וְכׇל־צְבָאָֽם׃ וַיְכַ֤ל אֱלֹהִים֙ ה׃

exactText: שָָֽֽׂ ֥ר עָ שֶׁ ל־מְלַאכְתּ֖וֹ אֲ כְׇּ בִיעִ֔י מִ שְָּׁ יּ֣וֹם הַ בַּ ת֙ בֹּ שְֶׁ יִּ֑ה וַ שָָֽׂ עָ בַת֙ שֶָׁ ֣י ב֤וֹ כְִּ אֹת֑וֹ שֶׁ ֖ דֵֵּ בִיעִ֔י וַיְקַ שְָּׁ ת־י֣וֹם הַ ךְ אֱלֹהִים֙ א וַיְבָ֤ר וֹת׃ שָֽֽׂ רָ֥א אֱלֹהִ֖ים לַעֲ בָּ ר־ שֶׁ ל־מְלַאכְתּ֔וֹ אֲ כְׇּ מִ And it was evening, and it was morning, the first day. And the heaven and earth were completed and all their multitudes. And God finished, on the seventh day, THEIR labor THEY had done, and rested on the seventh day from all THEIR labor THEY had done. And God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it, for on it THEY rested from all Their work that God had created to do.1 Hai Gaon permitted the usage of this extended version of the blessing over wine for the Qiddush on Pesaḥ.

exactText: הוּא אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ אֲ בֵל וְיָצַר לִמְתוּקֵי אָֽר תֵּ י נֵֶּ כִין מַעֲדַ מֵֵּ הַ ֽיעַ בִּ שְָֽׂ ן וְהִתְקִין עָסִיס מִן־הָעֲנָבִים לְהַ ֽפ גָּ מִן־הַ שֶׁירוֹ תִּ רָא בָּ מַח לִבּוֹ עַל שְָֽׂ וֹתָיו יִ שֶׁוֹקֵקָה שֶׁ שֶׁ ֽפ א נ לֵּ רְעֵבָה וּלְמַ שֶׁ ֽפ נ יו יְבָרְכוּ לְבוֹרְאֵֽהוּ… שָָֽׂ רִי מַעֲ פְֶּ Blessed are You, CAUSE our God, Sovereign of eternity, who created sweet wine and good aged wine from the grape plants, for it is pleasant for the soul and good for the human, makes the heart rejoice and the face shine, and is comforting to the mourners, and lets the embittered of soul forget their poverty and heals all who drink it (at least, who drink it within reason and sound mind). It is happiness to the heart, joy, and much mirth to those who drink it.

exactText: Our God created it of old, to please the works primally established, that all who drink it bless the Deity, and praise the Creator of Understanding, Who established the land’s delicacies, and made the earth’s sweets. This is our God, Who created aged wine from the vine, and established sweet wine from the grapes, to satiate the hungry soul, and to fill the parched soul. Drinker’s hearts will rejoice; over the fruits of God’s works they will bless their Creator. ָה וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ ךְ הָעוֹלָם ֽל אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ מ Blessed are You, CAUSE , our God, Sovereign of Eternity, שֶׁד קָָּ וֹ הָאֵל הַ דֵּ רוֹמָם לְבַ מְֵּ ךְ הַ ֽל מֵּ הַ Sovereign alone exalted, the holy Deity, ן ֽפ גָָּ רִי הַ פְֶּ בּוֹרֵא Creator of the vine-fruit. 1 Genesis 1:31–3:3 ב

exactText: Next, the sanctification of the day proper – Qiddush, on the next page. Recited to this day in Yemenite communities, this poetic rendition of the qiddush proper for Pesaḥ was approved of by Rav Saˤadia Gaon. The normative qiddush is printed in larger text. When larger text and smaller text are printed parallel, the larger text is elided when the poetic rendition is read. Elsewhere, it is incorporated into the poem. Passages in [brackets] are added on Friday night. ה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ ָה וֹיְהךְ הָעוֹלָם ֽל  אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ מ ר שֶׁ אֲ Blessed are You, CAUSE our God, Sovereign of Eternity, who… ל־עָם כְׇּ בָּחַר־בָּֽנוּ מִ ל־לָשוֹן כְׇּ וְרוֹמְמָֽנוּ מִ נוּ בְּמִצְוֺתָיו שֶָֽׁ דְֵּ וְקִ ל־עָם וְרוֹמְמָֽנוּ כְׇּ נוּ מִ שֶָֽׁ דְֵּ קִ ֽנוּ בָּ חַר־ בָּ ל־לָשוֹן כְׇּ מִ לֵֽנוּ רָצָה־בָנוּ וַיְפַאֲרֵֽנוּ׃

exactText: שָֽׂ A gift distinguished us from all peoples a pleasant land promised to us. THEY sanctified the name in the world entire, for the sake of the ancestors who did the divine will. Great deeds aplenty THEY did for them, and there is no accounting THEIR wonders. A sacred gathering THEY called us, a pleasant vineyard planted with merriment, And THEY called them treasured to THEIR name, took them first from all the earth’s nations, For they are parallel to the multitude Above, and accounted as the stars of heaven, And are highest in the midst of the land, and most honored of all the countries; Their faces glow like the face of the sun, and their appearance is likened to the ministering angels. To them kings will be in awe and arise, princes, and will bow.

exactText: On account of the CAUSE who is trusted, the Sanctity of Israel who chose them. All who see them will recognize them that they are a seed blessed by the CAUSE, THEY sanctified them with an eternal sanctity, and THEIR great Name THEY called over us. THEY called us a congregation to THEIR name, a treasure and portion from days of eternity. And THEY drew us near to Mount Sinai, congregated us before Ḥorev, and granted us words of life, written with the finger of THEIR splendor. And THEY did for us miracles and wonders, and saved us from our stressors, And redeemed us from the hand of the enemy, and rescued us from the hand of the bigot אַהֲבָה בְּ ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ וֹן־לָֽנוּ יְה תֵּ ֽתִּ וַ תוֹת בָּ שֶַׁ [ לִמְנוּחָה יםנִֶּ ים וּזְמַ גִָּ מְחָה חַ שִָֽׂ מוֹעֲדִים לְ ת־יוֹם וֹן א שָֽׂ שָָֽׂ לְ ת־יוֹם ה וְא זֶּ ת הַ בָּ שַָּׁ [ ]חַג ה זְמַן חֵירוּתֵנוּ זֶּ וֹת הַ צָּ מֵַּ הַ אַהֲבָה בְּ [ מִקְרָא ר לִיצִיאַת מִצְרָֽיִם׃

exactText: לְהוֹדִֽיעַ שֶָׁ לֵבָב בְּ וּגְבוּרוֹת לְאוֹהֲבָיו וְנִפְלָאוֹת רַבּוֹת לִבְנֵי יְדִידָיו׃ And THEY chose it on this day of all the days, and desired it and sanctified it of all occasions, to be praised on it for the wonders of THEIR works, to be commemorated on it year after year. To make known that on it the CAUSE took THEIR servants from Egypt, salvaged us from the iron furnace. To make known that on it they received THEIR rule as desired, to serve THEM wholeheartedly. To make known that on it THEY did vengeance on their enemies, and on it sunk their stressors in the sea. To make known that on it the CAUSE our God did miracles and great things for those who love THEM, and wonders aplenty for the children of those THEY cherished. ג

exactText: ים מִֵּ ל־הָעַ כְׇּ מִ תָּ שְֶׁ ֽ דֵַּ וְאוֹתָֽנוּ־קִ תָּ י בָֽנוּ־בָחַֽרְ כְִּ ַבָּת שֶׁ [ ךָ שְֶׁ וּמוֹעֲדֵי קׇדְ [ נוּ׃ תָּ וֹן הִנְחַלְ שָֽׂ שָָֽׂ מְחָה וְ שִָֽׂ בְּ For You chose us and sanctified us over all the peoples, and in [love and desire, in] happiness and joy apportioned Your [Sabbath and] sacred festivals to us. שֶׁ דֵֵּ ָה מְקַ וֹה יְה תָּ רוּך אַ בָּ בָּת וְ שַָּׁ [רָאֵל שְָֽׂ יִ ים׃נִֶּ מַ זְֶּ וְהַ Blessed are You, CAUSE, who sanctifies [the Sabbath and] Israel and the occasions. The following parts are added on Saturday nights, folowing the custom of YaQNeHaZ. Since YaQNeHaZ sounds like the German ”jag den Hase” – ”hunt the hare,” this is illustrated with a depiction of a hare hunt. The first Saturday night blessing, for the light — Ner.

And the seventh day from the six working-days You sanctified, distinguished and sanctified Your people Israel in holiness. ׃שֶׁ  לְקֹֽד שֶׁ ין קֹֽד בֵּ בְדִיל מֵַּ ָה הַ וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ Blessed are You, CAUSE, who distinguishes between holy and holy. exactText: וּרְחַץ And washU-rḥatz Wash the hands in purity complete, without a brakha, just being neat. Lava le mani con ogne mondezza senza far berahà, con politezza. לַאוַה לֵי מַאנִי קוֹן אוֹנְיִי אוֹנְדֵיצַה וֹלִיטֵיצַה׃ פֶּסֵינְצַה פַֿאר בְּרָכָהקוֹן From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 Traditional Ashkenazi practice is to wash without saying the blessing over washing hands. Sepharadi practice is to recite a blessing. If it is your custom to say a blessing, feel free to do so individually. Optional blessing: נ שֶָֽׁ דְֵּ ר קִ שֶׁ ךְ הָעוֹלָם אֲ ֽל ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ מ וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ מִצְוֺתָיו בְּ ֽנוּ עַל־נְטִילַת יָדָֽיִם: וָּ וִצִ Blessed are You, CAUSE our God, Sovereign of Eternity, who sanctified us with THEIR commandments and commanded us about washing hands. ַרַפְס כְּGreensKarafs Celery in vinegar you must infuse, and say the blessing as is done by us Jews.

exactText: Nel aceto dell'appio infonderai e bore peri ha-adama benedirai. ְיוֹ אִינְפֿוֹנְדֵירַאִי פֶּ נֵיל אַזֵיטוֹ דֵיל אַ ְרִי הָאֲדָמָה בֵּינֵידִירַאִי׃ פֶּאֵי בּוֹרֵא From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 Wait, did I spell that wrong? No. The vocalization of this word as “karpas” is a mistaken one, used in communities where Persian and Arabic were unknown. In Persian and Arabic, the word کرفس karafs refers to celery. In ancient times, a symposium meal would always start with appetizers and dips. Nowadays, this custom is preserved in the seder, but generally in a limited sense. But the purpose of the appetizers is to tide the guests over during the discussion! So my practice is to have a full crudité course here. We have on the table celery and other vegetables, along with an assortment of dipping sauces. We say the blessing below, and eat our amuse-bouche. ךְ הָעוֹלָם ֽל ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ מ וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ רִי הָאֲדָמָה. פְֶּ בּוֹרֵא Blessed are You, CAUSE our God, Sovereign of Eternity, creator of the fruit of earth. ה

exactText: יַחַץ HalvingYaḥatz Break the middle’s middle of the matza tray. To be the afiqoman it must be hidden away. Parti il scimùr di mezzo e mezzo di esso, sia per aficómèn riposto e messo. וֹסִּוּר דִי מֵיזוֹ אִי מֵיזוֹ דֵי אֵי מֵּ שִֶׁ ַארְטִּי אִיל פֶּ וֹּוֹסְטוֹ אֵי מֵיס פֶּן רִי ֵיר אֲפִיקוֹמ פֶּסִיאַה From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 We’re going to be adding some verses from the Tunisian practice that add some meaning to this practice. As I break the middle matza, anyone who wishes can recite in Tunisian Arabic: Thus the CAUSE tore the sea into twelve paths — when our ancestors the children of Israel went out from Egypt, by the hand of our master and prophet and noble Moshe son of Amram, peace be upon him — and desired their freeing from that slavery;

exactText: thus THEY will free us from this exile, for the sake of THEIR great and awesome name, and the children will return to their borders, amen, thus may it be willed. רְגׅ ִׄ טְרִיק חִין כ שֶָָׁא קְצִס ﭏַלָּה לְבְּחַָאר עַלָָא תְּנַעָָא דֵּ ְכְּהָָא ְנָָא וְנַבִּינָָא דְֵּרָָאֵל מִן מְצַָאר עַלָָא יֵד סִי שְָֽׂנָָא וְלָָאד יִ דֵּוּדֵּ ְׅאַג ְהוּם כְּ ִַׄיף פ כְּא ְָָׄלָָאם וְרְצ סִּ נָָא מוּסָה בִּן־עַמְרָם עַלֵיהִ אַ ְָׄרִיפ שֶׁ וְ ָָא דֵּ ְכְִּעְבּוּד׃ הַָא שֶׁמִן־הַָאדָָאךְּ ָלוּת לְמַעַן גָּ ְנָָא מִן־הָָאד לְ כְּ ֻׄיְפ ן יְהִי רָצוֹן׃ כְֵּ נִים לִגְבוּלָם אָמֵן בָּ בוּ שֶָׁ וֹרָא וְ נֶּ דוֹל וְהַ גָָּ מוֹ הַ שְֶׁ After this, we wrap the larger piece, the afiqoman, in a cloth napkin. A guest puts it on their shoulder, and walks at least six feet (four cubits, three meters), and recites in any preferred language: A reminder of our ancestors who left Egypt with their kneading-troughs bundled in their clothes on their backs.

exactText: ְאֲרוֹתָם צְרוּרוֹת שֶׁ ִצְרַֽיִם וּמִ מֵּ יָצְאוּ מִ שֶׁ ר לַאֲבוֹתֵֽינוּ זֵֽכ ִכְמָם׃ שִֶׁמְלוֹתָם עַל־ שָֽׂ בְּ ִיר לִ כְּ דְ ְנָָא לִּי דֵּוּדֵּגְ׳ כִֿרְג׳וּ מִן מְצַָאר פְנוֹם תַּ כְּתִיָיאבְּהוֹם עַלָא ִׄוְעֲגַ׳איינְהוֹם מַצְרוּר פ ׃ ִידגָּ מַ TellingMaggid Say the haggadah as written herein, until all of it’s said — with ha laḥma begin. Dirai la hagada come e qui scritto da ha lahma sin che el resto abbi ditto. דָה קוֹמֵי אֵי קְוִי סְקְרִיט גָָּ דִירַאִי לַה הַ דַא הָא לַחְמָא סִין קֵי אֵיל רֵיסְטוֹ אַבְּיִי דִיטוֹ׃ We uncover the matza, lift it up, and recite the following passage aloud: י אֲכָֽלוּ אַבְהָתָֽנָא דִֵּ הָא לַחְמָא עַנְיָא ׇל־דִכְפִין יֵיתֵי וְיֵיכֹל. כְּ אַרְעָא דְמִצְרָֽיִם׃ בְּ ׇל־דִצְרִיךְ יֵיתֵי וְיִפְסַח׃ כְּ This is the poor-bread that our ancestors ate in the land of Egypt. All who hunger should come and eat; all who need should come and paschalize. אַרְעָא בְּ אָה בָּ נָה הַ שֶָׁ א הָכָא. לְ תָּ שַָּׁ הָ א עַבְדֵי. תָּ שַָּׁ ְמִתְבַּנְיָא דֵּרָאֵל שְָֽׂ דְיִ נֵי חוֹרִין׃ בְּ אָה בָּ נָה הַ שֶָׁ לְ Now we are here, next year in the land of Israel [rebuilt]. Now we are slaves, next year we will be free. ו

exactText: ין בִּ בִין וּבֵין־מְסֻ שְֶׁ ין־יוֹ בֵּ ילוֹת אָֽנוּ אוֹכְלִין לֵּ כׇל־הַ בְּ שֶׁ ין׃ בִּ ֽנוּ מְסֻ לָּ כְֻּ ה זֶּ ֽיְלָה הַ לַּ הַ Sheb-khol ha-leylot ánu okhlin bein yoshvin u-vein m’subin — ha-láyla ha-zeh kullánu m’subin. Together we sing in English: What separates this one special night from all other nights? On all other nights we eat all our bread with yeast or without! On this night alone, on this night alone we only eat without. On all other nights we eat all our greens with any kind of taste! On this night alone, on this night alone just bitter greens. On all other nights we do not even have to dip our food at all! On this night alone, on this night alone we dip our veggies twice. On all other nights we sit at the dinner table any way we want!

exactText: On this night alone, on this night alone we all lean in our chairs. The youngest capable person — or anyone who needs to leave early — may recite the following passage, from the Yemenite practice, to the table (in whichever language is comfortable): English Hebrew Judeo-Arabic What tells this night from the rest of nights? ילוֹת לֵּ ׇל־הַ כְּ ה מִ זֶּ ֽיְלָה הַ לַּ ה הַ נֶָּ תַּ שְֶׁ נִֶּ מַה ר הַדִ׳ה אַל־לַּילַהּ מִן גְ׳מִיע בַּ מַא כְ׳ יִאַלִי? לְּ אַל־ Our ancestors and forefathers left Egypt the house of slavery where they were making straw into bricks, for whom? For Pharaoh the completely wicked, whose head was wide with insolence, and whose mouth was wide like an oven, and God brought down on the Egyptians: ית בֵּ צְרַֽיִם מִֵּ יָצְאוּ קַדְמוֹנֵֽינוּ וַאֲבוֹתֵֽינוּ מִ ן, ֽב תּ בַּ ים לְבָנִים שִָֽׂ הָיִֽינוּ עוֹ שֶׁ עְבּוּד שִָּׁ הַ ר שֶׁ מוּר, אֲ גָָּ ע הַ שֶָׁ לְמִי?

exactText: ֥ית בֵּ צְרַ֖יִם מִ מִֵּ ָ֛ה מִ וֹק יָ֗ד הוֹצִיאָ֧נוּ יְה חֹ֣ז בְּ עֲבָדִֽים׃ With a mighty hand the CAUSE brought us out from Egypt, from the slave-house.”7 אֱמַר נֶּ שֶׁ תַח לוֹ, פְֶּ תְּ אוֹל – אַ שְֶׁ אֵינוֹ יוֹדֵֽעַ לִ שֶׁ וְ ֗ה עֲב֣וּר ז בַּ יּ֥וֹם הַה֖וּא לֵאמֹ֑ר בַּ ֣ לְבִנְךָ֔ תָּ דְ גַָּ וְהִ צְרָֽיִם׃ מִֵּ צֵאתִ֖י מִ בְּ ָה֙ לִ֔י וֹ֤ה יְה שָָֽׂ עָ And the one who doesn’t how to ask, you open him up, as it is said, and you will tell your child on that day, saying, “On account of this the CAUSE did this to me when I went out of Egypt.”8 Let’s read a few different alternate takes on the four children before we continue. To start, a short satirical play I wrote in 2021 (with minor adjustments).

exactText: Our characters: the four children, the Bad Parent, and the narrator. The people who read the respective paragraphs for the four children will read the children below. We’ll take volunteers for the Bad Parent and the Narrator. Narrator: There are four children in each of us, and each child is two children, and each answer is two answers. We know the former, but the latter are here revealed. What are the children? Wise Child: A wise one — or a smart-ass! Wicked Child: A wicked one — or a free-thinker! Simple Child: A simple one — or a new learner! Doesn’t Know: And the one who doesn’t know how to ask. Narrator: What does the wise one say? Wise Child: What are the testimonies and laws and rulings that the CAUSE our God commanded? Narrator: In other words?

exactText: Wise Child: I just got back from college and I want to show off! Bad Parent: And I will respond with an obscure law for him to embarrass himself with. So, explain to me why we can’t eat dessert after midnight? Narrator: What does the wicked one say? Wicked Child: “What is this work to you?” Narrator: In other words? Wicked Child: I feel disconnected from the Jewish community. Bad Parent: And I will respond with an unhelpful insult. You’re not a real Jew! In other news, synagogue attendance is inexplicably dropping. Narrator: What does the simple one say? Simple Child: What’s this? Narrator: In other words? Simple Child: I never had access to a Jewish education, and I’m trying to learn the basics later in life! Bad Parent: And I will respond with a condescending, oversimplified explanation. Well once there was this wicked wicked man, and his name was Pharaoh! Can you say “Pharaoh?” Narrator: What does the one who does not know how to ask say? [beat] In other words? 7 Exodus 13:14 8 Exodus 13:8 י

exactText: Doesn’t Know: No soy hablante nativo del idioma, por lo que no puedo encontrar una comunidad judía que satisfaga mis necesidades. Bad Parent: And I will respond without helping. DO… YOU… SPEAK… ENGLISH!? Next,one of my favorite takes on the text, from Daniel Handler (aka Lemony Snicket)’s commentary in the New American Haggadah. Anyone who would like to read, feel free. Some scholars believe there are four kinds of parents as well. The Wise Parent is an utter bore. “Listen closely, because you are younger than I am,” says the Wise Parent, “and I will go on and on about Jewish history, based on some foggy memories of my own religious upbringing, as well as an article in a Jewish journal I have recently skimmed.” The Wise Parent must be faced with a small smile of dim interest.

exactText: The Wicked Parent tries to cram the story of our liberation into a set of narrow opinions about the world. “The Lord let us out of Egypt,” the Wicked Parent says, “which is why I support a bloodthirsty foreign policy and I'm tired of certain types of people causing problems.” The Wicked Parent should be told with a firm voice: “With a strong hand God rescued the Jews from bondage, but it was my own clumsy hand that spilled hot soup in your lap.” The Simple Parent does not grasp the concept of freedom. “There will be no macaroons until you eat all of your brisket,” says the Simple Parent at a dinner honoring the liberation of oppressed peoples. “Also, stop slouching at the table.” In answer to such statements, the Wise Child will roll his eyes in the direction of the ceiling and declare: “Let my people go.”

The verse says, “on that day.” If on that day, could it be while it is still light out? The verse says “on account of this.” — You’d only say “on account of this” when matza and maror is laid out before you!

exactText: And they began to worship false worship, and worshipped the stars and the orbits, and made them houses, and made within them images of wood and stone and of silver and gold, and thus began all the people. They came and assembled and ordered the matter for worship. And they said that the form would do good and evil. And from this the time went on and the days went on, and the name of Allah was forgotten from their mouths. And not one of them was left who knew the highest Allah, until Avraham our Father (peace be upon him) came. And he taught the people the worship of Allah and divine unity. Thus said the sages of blessed memory. ה אֲבוֹתֵינוּ הָי לָּ חִ תְּ נִי, מִ בְּ וְתֹאמַר לוֹ: אָמְרוּ שֶׁ מוֹ כְְּ עוֹבְדֵי צְלָמִים וְאוֹתוֹת, ,שֶׁהַחֲכָמִים זִכְרָם לִבְרָכָה, מֵימֵי אֱנוֹ דוֹלִים.

exactText: בְּ לַחֲכָׄמִים זִכְׄרָם לַ When Avraham our Father (peace be upon him) was born, the astrologers saw that one star rose up and swallowed three stars. They went to Nimrod the sultan, and said to him, “O our lord! At this very time a son is being born to Teraḥ. And see the great wonder regarding the heavens and constellations! For one star rose and swallowed three stars. And we know, o our lord, this is the child who will destroy the king and corrupt the religion!” לֵד אַבְרָהָם אָבִינוּ (עָלָיו וָּ הִ שֶׁ עֵת בְּ י קָם כְִּ לוֹם) רָאוּ הָאִצְטַגְנִינִים שָָּׁ הַ וֹכָבִים. כְּה שֶָׁ לוֹ שְֶׁ חָד וּבָלַע לִ וֹכָב א כְּ וּלְטָן וְאָמְרוּ לוֹ, סִּ ל־נִמְרוֹד הַ הָלְכוּ א ן בֵּ עָה נוֹלָד שָָּׁ אוֹתָהּ הַ בְּ “אֲדֹנֵנוּ! דוֹל עַל גָָּ א הַ ל פֶּ ת הַ רַח.

exactText: allege an allegory on this matter. An allegory of a horse to whom men said, ‘We will give you a bushel of barley, and come, we will cut off your head.’ The horse said to them, ‘What nonsense! If you cut off my head, who will eat the barley?’ And it’s like that here. If you kill my child, what benefit is a thousand gold to me?” They said to him, “Blessed to old age, this one will die and you will father another child in his place.” He said to them, “I know not if I will father one in his place or if I will not father one!” When he saw strength in his child, he locked him up and isolated him. There Avraham our father (peace be upon him) grew up, and he became a three-year-old.

exactText: He reasoned that if the sun were a god, then why would the light go into darkness for the moon and stars? He reasoned that if they were the creators of existence, then why would the light shine forth and the sun rise? He said (peace be upon him): “An intelligent man will verify for himself that they are created alike, and one higher than them willed to lead them both.” He grasped this by his own intelligence. God in divine glory appeared before him and said to him, “Avraham, how outstanding are you in knowledge of the heavens and the constellations! I am the One who created you and created them and created all the heavens and earth!” עְתּוֹ עַל דֵַּ ין עַל וִּ וְהוּא נִהְיָה מְצֻ ת לוֹת.

exactText: אָנָא הוּוָא אַלְדִי פִֿ אַסְמָא, וּ כְׄלַקְתַךּ וּכְׄלַקְתְהוֹם, וּכְׄלַקְתְ גְמִיע אַסְמָאוָאת וּלַארָאצִׄי!“ From that moment on, Avraham went against his father. And he said to him, “O my father, tell me please, who is the master who creates the world?” He said to him, “O my son, those idols that are in the house, they are the ones who created the world.” He said to him, “O my father, noble are their laws to me. Come, let us sacrifice an offering to them, that they may desire me.” There his father went into the house of the godless of wood and stone and silver and gold, there he went to his mother. And he said to her, “O my mother, get up, make me a great feast, and let us sacrifice it to the idols, that that they may desire me.”

exactText: If their mouth has no voice or strength, why do you worship them and abandon the Eternal God who created me and created you and created all the heavens and earth?” Said Nimrod to Avraham, “I am he who created you and created them and created all the heavens and earth!” He said to him, “O sir, if that is verifiably true, order the sun to rise from the west and set in the east! And can you do any such thing? exactText: free alive from judgement, he will destroy the king and corrupt the religion. We have no choice but to burn him!” When they could do the burning, they took Avraham our father (peace be upon him) there and cast him into a burning fiery oven. Then God in divine glory was revealed to them and saved him in kindness and magnificence, and he went out from there whole. From this all the people saw the miracle, and he tithed (peace be upon him) and taught the people the worship of Allah and divine unity. ט פֶַּ שְֶׁ מִֵּ חְרְרֵהוּ חַי מִ שֶַׁ “הַהוּא, אִם נְ חִית שְֶׁ ךְ וְיַ ל מֵּ ת־הַ ן, הוּא יַחֲרִיב א כְֵּ רָפֵהוּ!” שְָֽׂ א לִ לָּ ת. אֵין לָנוּ א דֵָּ ת־הַ א רֵפָה, שְָֽׂ וֹת שָֽׂ ר הָיָה לַעֲ שֶָׁ פְ ר א שֶׁ אֲ כְַּ ת־אַבְרָהָם אָבִינוּ (עָלָיו ם לָקְחוּ א שֶָׁ .

exactText: חַ גְָּ בִּ וּ קָא (עְלֵיהּ בְּ בּ, וּ גָּ אס עְ נֶָּ רָאת גְמִיע אַ אדַת ﭏֲלָהּ בָּ אס עִ נֶָּ לָאם) יְעַלַם אַ סְִּ אַ וּתוֹחִידוֹ. Thus said the sages of blessed memory, and added this gem: From guarding the faith of Avraham our father (peace be upon him), and from joining against all the world to join with his side, and from his knowledge of unity, on their account he was saved from his decree, and taught the people the worship of Allah and divine unity. And this is the proof — Way-yomer Yəhoshuaˤ el kol ha-ˤam . And this is the translation — And Yəhoshúaˤ said to all the people: Thus says the CAUSE God of Israel — across the river your ancestors dwelt of old, Teraḥ father of Avraham and Naḥor, and they served other gods.

exactText: ֣ן דֵָּ ֥ר יַעֲבֹ֖דוּ שֶׁ ֛וֹי אֲ גָּ ת־הַ נָֽה׃ וְגַ֧ם א שֶָׁ מֵא֖וֹת דֽוֹל׃ גָָּ שֶׁרְכֻ֥ בִּ אָנֹ֑כִי וְאַחֲרֵי־כֵ֥ן יֵצְא֖וּ hundred years. But also the nation for whom they will work I will judge, and after that they will go out with much wealth.”10 We cover the matza and lift the cup of wine. This next passage was written in 2024 by David Lau, the Ashkenazi chief rabbi of Israel, to be recited at this point in the seder, to pray for the redemption of hostages in Gaza. I have some issues with R. Lau, but there is no greater mitzvah than the liberation of hostages and I have no issues with the prayer itself so it is included below. May it be that I never need to include it again.

exactText: May it be the will of our Parent in Heaven who brought THEIR people Israel out from the suffering of Egypt that THEY bless and save our abducted brothers and sisters, bound with iron chains. May THEY strengthen their souls and faith, protect them from all harm and disease, have mercy on THEIR sons and daughters awaiting THEIR salvation, nullify all cruel decrees from upon them. In THEIR great kindness, may THEY hasten their redemption and may they quickly emerge from darkness to light, and from the pit of captivity to the freedom of the world, and return in peace to their families and to their homes. Please, plant brotherhood, peace and friendship in the hearts of all, remove envy and baseless hatred, and spread over us the Sukkah of Your peace, and may we merit to soon sing before You a New Song.

exactText: נֵרֵד וְנַעֲ בְרִית כְבוֹדִי, אֲנִי בִּ עַצְמִי, אֲנִי בְּ אֵרֵד אֲנִי שֶׁ ת עַד ֽר י מִתְקָר תִּ עְ דֵַּ ָה, אֲנִי הוּא וְלֹא אַחֵר׃ וֹתִי, אֲנִי יְה שָָּׁ קְדֻ בִּ תִי, אֲנִי לָּ גְדֻ בִּ Said our Sages of blessed memory — When the Holy Blessed One came down upon Egypt, nine thousand myriads came with him. Some were angels of fire, and some were angels of hail, and some were angels of tumult, and some were angels of trembling, and some were angels of quaking. And trembling and quaking would take hold of all who saw them. They said before THEM — “Master of the Universe, [God of Gods and Lord of Lords], is it not so that when sovereigns of flesh and blood goes down to battle, their nobles and servants surround them in their glory?

And Israel saw the great hand that the CAUSE did to Egypt, and the people revered the CAUSE and believed in the CAUSE and in Moshe THEIR servant. 35 How many were struck with a finger? Ten plagues. Say from this: in Egypt they were struck with ten plagues, and by the sea they were struck with fifty plagues. ה כְָּ ה וּמַ כְָּ ׇל־מַ כְּ שֶׁ יִן ֽ נֶַּ ר אוֹמֵר: מִ ֽז י אֱלִיע בִּ רַ צְרִים מִֵּ רוּךְ הוּא עַל־הַ בָּ שֶׁדוֹ קָָּ הֵבִיא הַ שֶׁ Rabbi Eliˤézer says: From where is it that each and every plague that the Holy Blessed One brought on the 34 Exodus 8:15 35 Exodus 14:31 כב

As it is written, THEY sent on them THEIR fierce anger, wrath and rage and trouble, a posse of evil angels.36 Wrath — one; and rage — two; and trouble — three; posse of evil angels — four. Say from this: in Egypt they were struck with forty plagues, and by the sea they were struck with two hundred plagues.

exactText: As it is written, THEY sent on them THEIR fierce anger, wrath and rage and trouble, a posse of evil angels. Fierce anger — one; wrath — two; and rage and — three; trouble — four; a posse of evil angels — five. Say from this: in Egypt they were struck with fifty plagues, and by the sea they were struck with two hundred and fifty plagues. קוֹם עָלֵֽינ מֵָּ מָה מַעֲלוֹת טוֹבוֹת לַ כְַּ How many degrees of good has the PLACE done for us! As we sing the following song, Dayeinu, take the green onions off the table and gently tap each other on the head with them. This custom, originating in the Persian communities, serves to remind us that “the leeks we had in Egypt” came with a heavy cost. I’ve italicized the word gently because I don’t want to have to break out the first-aid kit. אִֽ 🡰צְרַֽיִם מִֵּ הוֹצִיאָֽנוּ מִ 🡴ַיֵּֽינ דֵּ If God had only… brought us out from Egypt, וְלֹא 🡷 פָטִים שְֶׁ ם ה בָה שָָֽׂ עָ or had only… done judgment on them, אִֽ 🡴 🡴ַיֵּֽינ דֵּ וְלֹא 🡷 ם ה בֵאלֹהֵיה שָָֽׂ עָ or had only… judged their deities, אִֽ 🡴 36 Psalm 78:49 כג

exactText: וַ יָּ ת הַ זֶַּ בִּ ירַקְרַ֥ק חָרֽוּץ - זוֹ בִּ ֗יהָ בְרוֹת וְ֝א ם׃ תּוֹרֵ֤י יָּ ת הַ זֶַּ בִּ עֲדִ֣י עֲדָיִ֑ים - זוֹ בַּ ת מִצְרַיִם׃ זֶַּ בִּ בֹ֖אִי - זוֹ תָּ וַ ף - זוֹ ֽס כְָּ ֥וֹת הַ דֵּ ת מִצְרַיִם׃ עִ֖ם נְקֻ זֶַּ בִּ ֔ךְ - זוֹ לָּ ה־ שָֽׂ זָהָב֙ נַֽעֲ ם:יָּ ת הַ זֶַּ בִּ And from where is it derived that God gave us their wealth? As it is said: And they stripped Egypt .37 They made it like a depth without fish in it. Alternatively, they made it like a trap without birdseed in it. Why does the text cherish the plunder of the sea over the plunder of Egypt? Only that which was in the houses did they take in Egypt. But that which was in the tribute houses they took at the sea.

exactText: And you will say, it is the pesaḥ offering for the CAUSE who passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt when smiting Egypt, and saved our homes. And the people bowed and prostrated.41 Here we’ll add a piyyut traditionally recited in North African and Iraqi communities after the pesaḥ is mentioned. The acrostic spells out Aharon Kohen (“Aharon the Priest”) as an acrostic, but it is uncertain whether this is an authorial signature or a mystical reference to the Biblical figure. The communal refrain is va-amartem zevaḥ pesaḥ hu l-Adonai. And Arrange, O faithful, praise בַח שֶׁֽ מוּנִים עִרְכוּ אֱ֯ to God and slaughter the slaughter, ֽבַח לָאֵל וְטִבְחוּ ט and you will say, it is the pesaḥ offering ֨סַח פֶּ ֽבַח־ ֡ם ז תּ וַאֲמַרְ for the CAUSE. Va-amartem… ָ֗ה׃

exactText: עַל־ שֶׁאָֽנוּ אוֹכְלִים עַל־ שֶׁ ה זוֹ צָָּ מַ ל־אֲבוֹתֵֽינוּ לְהַחֲמִיץ שֶׁ צֵקָם בְּ יק פִֶּ א הִסְ לֹּ שֶׁ לָכִים מְֵּ ךְ מַלְכֵי הַ ֽל ם מ גְלָה עֲלֵיה נִֶּ שֶׁ עַד אפ֨וּ יֹּ אֱמַר — וַ נֶּ שֶׁ רוּךְ הוּא וּגְאָלָם בָּ שֶׁדוֹ קָָּ הַ ֖וֹת צָּ צְרַ֛יִם עֻגֹ֥ת מַ מִֵּ ֨ר הוֹצִ֧יאוּ מִ שֶׁ צֵ֜ק אֲ בָּ ת־הַ א צְרַ֗יִם וְלֹ֤א יָֽכְלוּ֙ מִֵּ ֣וּ מִ שֶׁ ֽי־גֹרְ כְִּ ֣י לֹ֣א חָמֵ֑ץ כְִּ ֽם׃ ֥וּ לָה שָֽׂ וְגַם־צֵדָ֖ה לֹא־עָ הַַּ לְהִתְמַהְמֵ֔ This matza that we eat, on account of what? On account of that our ancestors’ dough was not done leavening when the Sovereign, Sovereign of Sovereigns, the Holy Blessed One was revealed to them and redeemed them, as it is said: And they baked the dough that they had brought out from Egypt into matza-cakes, unleavened, for they were forced out of Egypt and could not wait, nor had they made provisions.42 The reader for the following paragraph holds up the maror.

exactText: וּם מַה שֶׁאָֽנוּ אוֹכְלִים עַל־ שֶׁ ה מָרוֹר ז י אֲבוֹתֵֽינוּ יֵּ ת־חַ צְרִים א מִֵּ רְרוּ הַ מֵֵּ שֶׁ וּם שֶׁעַל־ ֜ם יהיֵּ ת־חַ אֱמַר — וַיְמָרְר֨וּ א נֶּ שֶׁ מִצְרַֽיִם בְּ ר֙ וּבִלְבֵנִ֔ים וּבְכׇל־עֲבֹדָ֖ה חֹ֙מ בְּ ֗ה שֶָׁ עֲבֹדָ֣ה קָ בַּ ֖ם ר־עָבְד֥וּ בָה שֶׁ ׇל־עֲבֹ֣דָתָ֔ם אֲ כְּ ֑ה אֵ֚ת ד שְָּׂ בַּ ךְ׃ פָֽר בְּ This maror that we eat, on account of what? On account of that the Egyptians embittered the lives of our ancestors in Egypt, as it is said: And they embittered their lives with hard work, with pitch and bricks and all sorts of field work, all the work that they worked for them crushingly.43 In many medieval haggadot, an illustration is included of a husband pointing at his wife when discussing maror, because what’s more embittering than your wife, am I right, folks? This is proof that boomer comics have existed since long before their eponymous generation. I’ve decided not to include one, but I will point out that in the 15th century Hileq and Bileq Haggadah the wife responds in kind, and also is next to the knives. 42 Exodus 12:39 43 Exodus 1:14 כז

exactText: You might notice an addition to the Seder plate that isn’t part of the traditional number. This is the Fish of Miriam! The Fish of Miriam is a millennium-old tradition, recorded by Rav Sherira Gaon in 10th- century Iraq. He would always have three cooked foods on the seder plate. • The egg, a product of the birds of the sky, a sign of renewal and rebirth, represented Moshe, the law, the heavens, and the revelational aspects of faith. • The bone, a product of the animals of the field, a commemoration of the original Pesaḥ sacrifice, represented Aharon, the priesthood, the earth, and the ritual aspects of faith. • And the fish, a product of the constantly flowing water, represented Miriam, prophecy, the waters, and the spiritual aspects of faith.

The 🐣 birds of the skies and the 🐟 fish of the seas, traveling the path of the sea.

exactText: בַּ שְֶׁ ֥ר נִ שֶׁ ץ אֲ ת־הָאָ֔ר לָ֙נוּ֙ א Not our ancestors alone has the Holy Blessed One redeemed, but us too THEY redeemed with them, as it is said — And us THEY saved from there, so as to bring us to give us the land THEY promised our ancestors.47 We cover the matza, lift up our cups, and recite: ֽחַ בֵּ שֶַׁ ל לְ לֵּ בִים לְהוֹדוֹת לְהַ יָּ לְפִיכָךְ אֲנַֽחְנוּ חַ ס לְמִי לֵּ ה וּלְקַ לֵּ ר לְבָרֵךְ לְעַ דֵֵּ לְפָאֵר לְרוֹמֵם לְהַ ים הָאֵלּוּ׃ סִִּ ל־הַנִ כְָּ ת־ ה לַאֲבוֹתֵֽינוּ וְלָֽנוּ א שָָֽׂ עָ שֶׁ מְחָה שִָֽׂ גוֹן לְ יָּ הוֹצִיאָֽנוּ מֵעַבְדוּת לְחֵרוּת מִ Thus we must thank, praise, laud, exult, exalt, glorify, bless, esteem, and acclaim the One who did for our ancestors and for us all these miracles. exactText: Mi-mizraḥ shémesh ˤad məvo’o məhullal shem Adonai! עַד־מְבוֹא֑ שֶׁ ֥מ שֶׁ זְרַח־ מִֵּ מִ ָֽה׃ וֹ֣ם יְה שֵֶׁ ֗ל לָּ מְ֝הֻ High over all nations, the CAUSE, over heaven THEIR glory! exactText: When Israel was leaving Egypt, Yaˤaqov’s house from a barbaric people, B-tzet Yisrael mi-Mitzráyim, beit Yaˤaqov me-ˤam loˤez, צְרָ֑יִם מִֵּ רָאֵל מִ שְָֽׂ צֵ֣את יִ֭ בְּ ֥ית יַ֝עֲקֹ֗ב מֵעַ֥ם לֹעֵֽז׃ בֵּ Judah became THEIR holy one, Israel THEIR dominion. Hayta Yəhuda l-qodsho, Yisrael mamshlotav. ֑שֶׁ הָיְתָ֣ה יְהוּדָ֣ה לְקׇדְ לוֹתָֽיו׃ שְֶׁ רָאֵ֗ל מַמְ שְָֽׂ יִ֝ The sea saw and fled, the Jordan turned backwards. Ha-yam ra’a va-yanos, ha-Yarden yissov l-aḥor. נֹ֑סיָּ֣ם רָ֭אָה וַ יָּ הַ ֥ב לְאָחֽוֹר׃ סִֹּ֗ן יִ דֵֵּ רְ יַּ הַ֝ The mountains danced like rams, the hills like lambs. He-harim raqdu kh-eylim, gəvaˤot ki-vnei tson. ֭הָרִים רָקְד֣וּ כְאֵילִ֑ים ה בְנֵי־צֹֽאן׃ כְִּ ֝בָע֗וֹת גְָּ What’s up, sea, that you fled, Jordan, that you turned backwards? Ma ləkha ha-yam ki tanus, ha-Yarden tissov l-aḥor? ֣י תָנ֑וּס כְִּ ם יָּךָ֣ הַ֭ לְּ מַה־ ֥ב לְאָחֽוֹר׃

exactText: סִֹּ תִּ ֗ן דֵֵּ רְ יַּ הַ֝ Mountains, that you danced like rams, hills like lambs? He-harim tirqdu kh-eylim, gəvaˤot ki-vnei tson? רְקְד֣וּ כְאֵילִ֑ים תִּ ֭הָרִים ה בְנֵי־צֹֽאן׃ כְִּ ֝בָע֗וֹת גְָּ Before the Master the earth quakes, before the One God of Yaˤaqov, Mi-lifnei Adon ḥúli áretz, mi-lifnei Elóah Yaˤaqov. ץ פְנֵ֣י אָ֭דוֹן ח֣וּלִי אָ֑ר לִּ מִ יַעֲקֹֽב׃ הַַּ פְנֵ֗י אֱל֣וֹ לִּ מִ֝ Who turns the rock into a water-pool, the flint to a water-spring.49 Ha-hofkhi ha-tzur agam máyim, ḥallamish l-maˤyno máyim. ֣וּר אֲגַם־מָ֑יִם צָּ הַהֹפְכִ֣י הַ לְמַעְיְנוֹ־מָֽיִם׃ שֶׁמִ֗י לָּ חַ֝ Next, we will be reciting an extended poetic version of the conclusion-blessing of the Maggid, found in the siddur of Rav Sa ˤadia and the Yemenite rites among others. For those who prefer the normative Ashkenazi text, it is included within the extended text, written in larger print.

exactText: Our ancestors were within the land of Egypt, suffering and laboring under Pharaoh king of Egypt’s hand. But they were fruitful and multiplied like earth’s dust, and grew in stature like the Lebanon cedars. You redeemed our ancestors from Egypt, with a strong hand and an outstretched arm. Our ancestors were within the land of Egypt, suffering and laboring under Pharaoh king of Egypt’s hand. But they were fruitful and multiplied like earth’s dust, and grew in stature like the Lebanon cedars. The seed of Yeshurun wore groaning, for the hand of the enemy was high over them. The Living and Enduring heard their voice, the Sovereign, Sovereign of sovereigns, the Holy Blessed One, gave ear to them. A good and upright one THEY raised from them, beloved Moshe in a vision was sent to them.

exactText: Together he showed them the signs of Shaddai, and with distinct plagues the Ḥamites were judged. All their firstborns were given to the slaughter, the first of their wombs to a great disease. Thus a great cry fell within the land of Egypt, weeping and mourning in all her courtyards. From slavery to freedom the beloved ones went out, and none failed from the ranks of their tribes. The Rock of Eternities led them in THEIR goodness, and brought them to the Reed Sea in great compassion. The sea closed them in with its vast waves, the bigot pursued with his vast force. They raised their eyes to the Heights, to beg for compassion from Jacob’s Might. The redeemed of the CAUSE opened their mouths, and praised their Sovereign for THEIR many wonders.

exactText: The mounts of the people’s enemies neighed to throw the bigots into the depths. The CAUSE split the waters of the Reed Sea and fulfilled THEIR oath that THEY swore to our ancestors. The beloved ones saw the bigots’ corpses, for they were lain out on the shore of the sea. Song and hymn and words of praise said Moshe our master, peace be upon him, before his Maker. Praises and glory and words of laudation said the liberated before their Redeemer and Rescuer. יעֵֽנ גִָּ ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ וֵאלֹהֵי אֲבוֹתֵֽינוּ יַ וֹן יְה כְֵּ אִים לִקְרָאתֵנוּ בָּ לְמוֹעֲדִים וְלִרְגָלִים אֲחֵרִים הַ ים שִָֽׂ שָָֽׂ ֽךָ וְ בִנְיַן עִיר בְּ מֵחִים שְָֽׂ לוֹם שֶָׁ לְ בָחִים זְֶּ ם מִן־הַ שֶָׁ ֽךָ׃ וְנֹאכַל עֲבוֹדָת בַּ מָם עַל־קִיר דֵָּ ֽיעַ גִָּ ר יַ שֶׁ סָחִים אֲ פְֶּ וּמִן־הַ יר חָדָש שִֶׁ ה לְךָ חֲךָ לְרָצוֹן וְנוֹד בַּ מִזְ ֽנוּ׃

exactText: שֵֶׁ דוּת נַפְ פְֶּ תֵֽנוּ וְעַל־ לָּ אֻ גְָּ עַל־ רָאֵל׃ שְָֽׂ אַל יִ גָָּ ָה וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ Thus, CAUSE our God and God of our ancestors, bring us to other festivals and pilgrimages to come to greet us in peace, rejoicing in the building of Your city and celebrating in Your labor. And we will eat there of the sacrifices and paschal offerings from which their blood is brought onto Your altar wall as desired, and we will thank You with a new song for our redemption and the liberation of our souls. Blessed are You, CAUSE, who redeemed Israel. לא

We recline to the left and drink our second cup. רׇחְצָה WashingRoḥtza Once again, wash your hands. With a brakha this time, without idle plans. Un'altra volta lavate le mani con berahà senza pensieri vani. אוּנַאלְטְרַה ווֹלְטַה לַאוַאטֵי לֵי מַאנִי ֵינְסְיֵירִי וַאנִי׃ פֶּקוֹן בְּרָכָה סֵינְצַה From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 We wash with a blessing. ר שֶׁ ךְ הָעוֹלָם אֲ ֽל ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ מ וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ נוּ שֶָֽׁ דְֵּ קִ מִצְוֺתָיו בְּ ֽנוּ עַל־נְטִילַת יָדָֽיִם: וָּ וִצִ Blessed are You, CAUSE our God, Sovereign of Eternity, who sanctified us with THEIR commandments and commanded us about washing hands.

exactText: מוֹצִיא ה צָָּ מַ Blessings over the Matza Motzi Matza Pick up the matzot on top of the set. Say ha-motzi leḥem but don’t eat it yet! But break that half, over which is quoth: ˤal akhilat matza — now eat them both! Piglia il scimùr di sopra e ci farai hamozzi lehem, e non ne mangiarai. Ma spezza quella mezza e detto poi gnal ahilat mazza — mangia ambi doi. ַארַאִי פְֶּרַה אֵי צִי פֶּוּר דִי סוֹ מֵּ ִשִֶׁילִיַיה אִיל פֶּ ם אֵי נוֹן נֵי מַאנְיַארַאִי׃ ח וֹצִיא ל מֵּ הַ וֹאִי פֵֶּיצַה קְוֵילַה מֵיזַה אֵי דֵיטוֹ פֶּ מַה סְ וֹאִי׃ דֵָּה מַאנְיַיה אַמְבִּי צָּ עַל אֲכִילָת מַ From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 Holding the two-and-a-half matzot in order, we say the following two blessings. ךְ הָעוֹלָם ֽל ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ מ וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ ץ ם מִן־הָאָר ֽח וֹצִיא ל מֵּ הַ Blessed are You, CAUSE our God, Sovereign of Eternity, who brings bread from the earth. ר שֶׁ ךְ הָעוֹלָם אֲ ֽל ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ מ וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ נוּ שֶָֽׁ דְֵּ קִ מִצְוֺתָיו בְּ ה צָָּ ֽנוּ עַל־אֲכִילַת מַ וָּ וִצִ Blessed are You, CAUSE our God, Sovereign of Eternity, who sanctified us with THEIR commandments and commanded us about eating matza. We break the top two matzot into pieces the size of an olive, add salt, and eat while reclining. (Note: my custom is to use the “rationalist’s k’zayit” — i.e. the actual size of an actual olive.) לב

exactText: מָרוֹר MarorMaror In ḥaroset you’ll make romaine be infused. Saying ˤal akhilat maror, as it is used. Nel haróssèt lattuca avrai infusa con dir gnal ahilat maror che s’usa. ת לַטוּקַה אַוְורַאִי אִינְפֿוּסַה נֵיל חֲרוֹס קוֹן דִיר עַל אֲכִילָת מָרוֹר קֵי סְ אוּסַה׃ From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 We each take about an olive’s worth of maror — originally romaine, in many communities horseradish. (The Shulḥan ˤArukh says not to use ground or processed maror for this step.) Take the vegetable, dip it in the ḥaroset provided, shake off the extra, and say: ר שֶׁ ךְ הָעוֹלָם אֲ ֽל ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ מ וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ נוּ שֶָֽׁ דְֵּ קִ מִצְוֺתָיו בְּ ֽנוּ עַל־אֲכִילַת מָרוֹר. וָּ וִצִ Blessed are You, CAUSE our God, Sovereign of Eternity, who sanctified us with THEIR commandments and commanded us about eating maror.

exactText: Eat sitting straight up. Take a bit of maror and mix it into the discarded wine in the disposable cup. (This is going somewhere, I promise!) וֹרֵ כְּSandwichKorekh The third matza fold like a book. And zekher la-miqdash with a reluctant look. Del terzo scimùr volgi in una foglia e di zeher lamicdasc di mala voglia. וּר ווֹלְגִיי אִין אוּנַה פֿוֹלְיַיה מֵּ ִשֶׁדֵיל טֵירְצוֹ ִי מַאלַה ווֹלְיַיה׃ דֵּ שֶׁ ָדֵּ ִקְ מֵּ ר לַ אֵי דִי זֵכ From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 We take a portion of matza (traditionally from the third, but I have extra), and a portion of maror (I use the ground horseradish here, since we’ve already fulfilled the requirement above) and, adding ḥaroset to taste, make a sandwich. We say, without a blessing: זְמַן בִּ ל לֵּ ה הִ שָָֽׂ ן עָ כְֵּ ל.

exactText: לֵּ הִ כְְּ שֶׁ דֵָּ קְ מִֵּ ר לַ זֵֽכ וֹרֵךְ כְּם — הָיָה יָּ הָיָה קַ שֶׁ דֵָּ קְ מִֵּ ית הַ בֵּ שֶׁ ם מַה יֵּ יַֽחַד לְקַ בְּ ה וּמָרוֹר וְאוֹכֵל צָָּ ֽסַח פֶּ[ ֥וֹת וּמְרֹרִ֖ים יֹאכְלֻֽהוּ׃ צָּ אֱמַר — עַל־מַ נֶּ שָּׁ A remembrance of the Temple per Hillel. Thus Hillel would do when the Temple was standing: he would sandwich [the pesaḥ offering], matza and maror and eat them together, to fulfill what was said: upon matza and maror-herbs they will eat it. ֻלְחָן עוֹרֵ שֶׁTablespreadShulḥan ˤOrekh Set the table, stuff your face with the food. And eat as much as you feel in the mood. Concia la mensa e il cibo in bocca caccia e mangia quanto voi che pro ti faccia. קוֹנְצִיַיה לַה מֵינְסַה אֵי אִיל צִיבּוֹ אִין בּוֹקַה קַאצִיַה ְרוֹ טִי פַֿאצִיַה׃ פֶּאֵי מַאנְיַיה קְוַאנְטוֹ ווֹאִי קֵי From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 We start with EGG WAR... on the next page. לג

exactText: I’ll pass out your equipment — one unpeeled hard-boiled egg. The rules of the game are simple: • Take your hard-boiled egg and tap it against your neighbor’s hard-boiled egg. • The goal is to crack your neighbor’s egg while leaving your egg uncracked. • The winner goes on to the next round. • The winner of the tournament is declared EGG WINNER and gets to eat first. • Take some of the discarded eggshell and mix it in to the disposable cup. (I swear this has a point! Okay now that that’s over we eat! צָפוּן HiddenTsafun The afiqoman, already set aside, in the end will be eaten in devotion and pride. L’aficómèn che già hai riservato in fin, con devozion’ sarà mangiato. ן קֵי גְיַה אַאִי רִיסֵירְבַאט לְאֲפִיקוֹמ אִין פִֿין קוֹן דֵיווֹצִיוֹן סַארַה מַאנְיַאטוֹ׃

exactText: From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 Make sure you’re full, because this is the last food we eat until tomorrow! We retrieve the afiqoman and say: בַע׃ וֹֹׁ אֱכַל עַל־הָשֽ ֽסַח הָנ פֶּ ן בָּ ר לְקׇרְ זֵֽכ A remembrance of the pesaḥ sacrifice that was eaten when full. We eat it reclining. בָּרֵ BlessBarekh Without eating or drinking anything more, Bentsh with a zimmun, at least three or four. Senza mangiar’ né ber’ altro procura dir berahà e per zimmun pon’ cura. ְרוֹקוּרַה פֶּסֵינְצַה מַאנְיַיאר נֵי בֵּיר אַלְטְרוֹ וֹן קוּרָה׃ פֶּוּן מֵּ ֵיר זִ פִֶּיר בְּרָכָה אֵי דֵּ From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 We pour the third cup and recite the birkat ha-mazon. Included within are poetic additions from the Cairo Geniza. Originally these poems would have replaced the entire birkat ha-mazon, but here they are nserted before the concluding verses of each paragraph.

exactText: A song of the stairs. When the CAUSE would return the returning of Zion we would be like dreamers. Thus full would be our mouths with laughter and our tongues joy; thus the nations will say, “The CAUSE has done greatness with them!” The CAUSE has done greatness Shir ha-ma ˤalot. B- shuv Adonai et shivat Tziyyon hayínu k- ḥolmim. Az yimmale səḥoq pínu u-lshonénu rina; az yomru va- goyim higdil Adonai laˤasot ˤim éleh. Higdil Adonai laˤasot ˤimmánu; hayínu səmeḥim. With permission of our teachers and masters and friends, let us bless [with 10: the God] from Whom we ate. Leader: אוכלים ומנהיג חוזר׃ רוּךְ בָּ [ אָכַֽלְנ שֶׁ לוֹ וּבְטוּבוֹ חָיִֽינוּ שָּׁ מִ Barukh [with 10 Elohéinu] she-akhálnu mi-shelo, uv-tuvo ḥayyínu. Bless [with 10: the God] from Whom we ate, and in Whose goodness we live! Eaters, leader repeats: הכל׃ְמוֹ׃ שֶׁרוּךְ הוּא וּבָרוּךְ בָּ Barukh hu u-varukh shəmo! Blessed be, blessed be THEIR name! exactText: מִיד לְעוֹלָם וָע תָּ ל־חַי כְׇּ פִי בְּ We thank You, CAUSE our God, for the sweet and good and wide land that You bequeathed to our ancestors, and for You, CAUSE our God, having brought us out of Egypt, and freed us from the slave-house, and for Your covenant that You engraved in our flesh, and or the life, grace, and mercy that You graced us, and for the eating of the food that You feed and provide us for always every day, every time, and every hour. For all that, CAUSE or God, we thank You and bless You; blessed be Your name in the mouths of all life always forever and ever. טָׄבַח אוֹנֵי לוּדִים ית עֲבָדִים בֵּ ר מִ שֶׁ עֹֽ בְּ רוּכִים פְֶּ יָׄצְאוּ Slaughtering the first-virility of the Lydians, they went out crushed with wealth from the slave-house. All say “Glory!” and there stand in awe — thus THEY redeemed them from enslaving. From stresses You brought them out, the souls that You freed. Eternal the covenant You cut with them, for the law and land that You bequeathed them. יִרְאָה עוֹמְדִים בְּ ם שֶָׁ בוֹד וְ כְָּ ׄלּוֹ אוֹמֵר כְֻּ עֲבִידִים׃ מֵַּ אָלָם מִ גְָּ לָׄכֵן ה הוֹצֵֽאתָ תָּ רוֹת אַ צָָּ מִׄ דִֽיתָ פֶָּ ר שֶׁ וֹת אֲ שֶׁ נְׄפָ תָּ רַֽ כְָּ רִית לָֽמוֹ בְּ ֽׄלָה ס׃ תָּ ר אוֹתָם הִנְחַֽלְ שֶׁ ץ אֲ ת וָאָֽר דֵָּ עֲׄבוּר 53 Exodus 12:27 54 Psalm 135:16 לו

exactText: Our God, our Parent, shepherd us, feed us, provide for us, sustain us, and relieve us, and make relief for us, CAUSE our God, speedily from all stresses, and please do not make us needy, CAUSE our God, neither for the gifts of flesh and blood nor for their loans, but only from Your hand, full and open and holy and wide, that we not be shamed or disgraced ever and ever, ֽיךָ וּבְמִצְוַת יוֹם מִצְוֺת בְּ ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ וֹרְצֵה וְהַחֲלִיצֵֽנוּ יְה דוֹלגָָּ ה י יוֹם ז כְִּ ה זֶּ הַ שֶׁדוֹ קָָּ דוֹל וְהַ גָָּ ת הַ בָּ שַָּׁ בִיעִי הַ שְָּׁ הַ אַהֲבָה בְּ בׇּת־בּוֹ וְלָנֽוּחַ־בּוֹ שְֶׁ ֽיךָ לִ  הוּא לְפָנ שֶׁוְקָדוֹ אלֹּ שֶׁ ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ וֹךָ וּבִרְצוֹנְךָ הָנִֽיחַ־לָֽנוּ יְה ֽ מִצְוַת רְצוֹנ כְְּ ָה וֹיוֹם מְנוּחָתֵֽנוּ וְהַרְאֵנוּ יְה בְּ תְהֵא צָרָה וְיָגוֹן וַאֲנָחָה לַֽיִם עִיר שֶָׁ ֽךָ וּבְבִנְיַן יְרוּ חָמַת צִיּוֹן עִיר נ בְּ אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ חָמוֹת׃

exactText: נֶּ וּעוֹת וּבַֽעַל הַ שֶֽׁעַל הַיְ בַּ ה הוּא תָּ י אַ כְִּ ֽךָ שֶׁ קׇדְ On Shabbat add: Desire and strengthen us, CAUSE our God, with Your commandments, and with the commandment of the great and holy seventh Sabbath day, for this day is great and holy before You to rest on it and relax on it in love as the commandments of Your will, and in Your will let us rest, CAUSE our God, that there be no stress or sorrow or moaning on the day of our resting, and show us, CAUSE our God, the comforting of Zion Your city and the building of Jerusalem Your holy city for You are a Master of salvation and a Master of comfort. 55 Deuteronomy 8:8 לז

exactText: תָּ וּן וְרַחוּם אָֽ נֶּ ךְ חַ ֽל י אֵל מ כְִּ ֽיךָ עֵינֵֽינוּ אֵל Our God and God of our ancestors, may these ascend and come and arrive and be seen and be desired and be heard and be accounted and be remembered: our memories and our accounts, and the memory of our ancestors, and the memory of the anointed son of David Your servant, and the memory of Jerusalem Your holy city, and the memory of all Your people the house of Israel before you, for survival, for goodness, for grace, for kindness, and for compassion, for life, and for peace, on this pilgrimage of matza. Remember us on it, CAUSE our God, for good, and account us on it for blessing, and save us on it for life.

exactText: And by word of salvation and compassion, have mercy and grace and compassion on us and save us, for our eyes turn to You, for a merciful compassionate Sovereign God are You. מְהֵרָה בְיָמֵֽינוּ׃ בִּ שֶׁ ֽד קָֹּ לַיִם עִיר הַ שֶָׁ וּבְנֵה יְרוּ And build Jerusalem, Your holy city, speedily in our days. ירִים שִֶׁ רְרוּ שֶֹׁׄדוּיִים פְֶּ רִים בָּ שְֶׁ מִ בְּ טֻבְעוּ כְְּ צׄוֹעֲנִים Freed, they sang songs as the Tanites drowned in the breaking-waves. Your communities went out in songs, singing cheer and praise. Boundary-Setter, free us on this day, as You — awesome and terrific — freed. Build there the city, from the day you will delight us with the joy of this day.

exactText: הוּא יִמְלוֹךְ עָלֵֽינוּ לְעוֹלָם וָע May the Compassionate One… reign over us forever and ever! הָרַחֲמָן ץ! מַֽיִם וּבָאָֽר שָָּׁ בַּ רַךְ בָּ הוּא יִתְ May the Compassionate One… be blessed in heaven and earth! הָרַחֲמָן וֹרִים דֵּח לְדוֹר בַּ תַּ שְֶׁ הוּא יִ ֽנוּ לָעַד וּלְנֵֽצַח נְצָחִים בָּ אַר פֶָּ וְיִתְ ֽנוּ לָעַד וּלְעוֹלְמֵי עוֹלָמִים! בָּ ר דֵַּ וְיִתְהַ May the Compassionate One… be lauded in all generations, glorified by us forever to all infinities, acclaimed by us forever to all eternities! הָרַחֲמָן כָבוֹד! בְּ הוּא יְפַרְנְסֵֽנוּ May the Compassionate One… provide for us honorably! הָרַחֲמָן ארֵֽנוּ וָּ צַָּ ֽנוּ מֵעַל לֵּ בּוֹר עֻ שְֶׁ הוּא יִ וְהוּא יוֹלִיכֵֽנוּ קוֹמְמִיּוּת לְאַרְצֵֽנוּ! May the Compassionate One… break the yokes from off our necks, and lead us unified to our land! הָרַחֲמָן ה זֶּ ֽיִת הַ בַּ בַּ ה בָּ רָכָה מְרֻ בְּ לַח־לָֽנוּ שְֶׁ הוּא יִֽ אָכַֽלְנוּ עָלָיו!

exactText: the CAUSE blesses THEIR people with peace.65 ךְ הָעוֹלָם ֽל ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ מ וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ ן׃ ֽפ גָָּ רִי הַ פְֶּ בּוֹרֵא Blessed are You, CAUSE our God, Sovereign of Eternity, Creator of the vine-fruit. We drink the third cup while reclining. הַלֵּל PraiseHallel We start with Shfókh and with Hallel we’re ending. Bless the One who saved you with a hand extending! Scefóch comincia ed il hallél finisci e quel che ti a salvo benedisci. ִי שְֶׁפוֹךְ קוֹמִינְצִיַיה אֵיט אִיל הַלֵּל פִֿינִי שֶׁ ִי׃ שֶׁאי קְוֵיל קֵי טִי אַה סַאלְוַטוֹ בֵּינֵידִי From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 Oddly enough for a section called praise, we start with curses. A question for discussion: why do you think that is? We pour the fourth cup, as well as the cups for Elijah and Seraḥ.

exactText: Okay, so remember that disposable cup with the drips of plague-wine and the maror and eggshells? I call it the Cup of Curses. This is my ”suitable for a fifth-floor apartment” in Jerusalem adaptation of a custom recorded among the Jews of Kurdistan: “In Oshno they dip the index finger in wine for each of the Ten Plagues… They sprinkle the drop in an empty eggshell and they also add a little arak, tobacco and maror. One of the men takes the egg and throws it quietly at the threshold of a Kurd who is known as a Jew-hater. He returns quietly and washes his face and hands before he continues to participate in the Seder.” We need a volunteer to flush it down the toilet in honor of the antisemites y”sh who deserve it *, then wash their hands and return to the table.

exactText: While the door is open, we sing songs for Elijah and Seraḥ: Eliyáhu the Prophet, Eliyáhu the Tishbite, Eliyáhu the Gileadite. Speedily in our days may he come with the Messiah son of David. Eliyáhu ha-navi. Eliyáhu ha-tishbi. Eliyáhu, Eliyáhu, Eliyáhu ha-gilˤadi. י בִּ שְֶׁ תִּ ֽהוּ הַ יָּ אֵלִ ׃ בִיא נֶָּ ֽהוּ הַ יָּ אֵלִ לְעָדִי׃ גִָּ ֽהוּ הַ יָּ אֵלִ ֽהוּ יָּ ֽהוּ, אֵלִ יָּ אֵלִ Bi-mhera v-yaménu, yavo elénu ˤim Mashiáḥ ben David, ˤim Mashiáḥ ben David. יָבוֹא אֵלֵֽינ ׃ מְהֵרָה בְּיָמֵֽינוּ בִּ וִד׃ דֵָּ ן־ בּ ֽיחַ שִֶׁ עִם־מָ וִד, דֵָּ ן־ בּ ֽיחַ שִֶׁ עִם־מָ Daughter of Asher, Seraḥ her name. Daughter of Asher who saw. Daughter of Asher, the daughter who saved a city in her wisdom. She comforted our patriarch, and will comfort us with her explanations of the Teaching and her new song.

exactText: ָ֑ה וֹיְ֭בָרֵךְ יִרְאֵ֣י יְה דֹלִֽים׃ גְָּ ֗ים עִם־הַ נִֶּ טַ קְָּ הַ֝ Yəvarekh yir’ei Adonai, ha-qtanim im ha-gdolim. THEY bless the reverent of the CAUSE, the small and the great! ֑ם ָ֣ה עֲלֵיכ וֹיֹסֵ֣ף יְה ֽם׃ נֵיכ בְּ ֗ם וְעַל־ עֲ֝לֵיכ Yosef Adonai ˤaleikhem; ˤaleikhem v-ˤal bəneikhem. May the CAUSE increase you all, you and your children too! ָ֑ה וֹם לַיה תּ רוּכִ֣ים אַ֭ בְּ ץ׃ מַ֥יִם וָאָֽר שֶָׁ ֗ה שֵָֽׂ עֹ֝ Bərukhim atem l-Adonai; óseh shamáyim va-áretz. Blessed are you all to the CAUSE, maker of heaven and earth! ָ֑ה וֹ֭מַיִם לַיה שֶָׁ מַ֣יִם שָָּׁ הַ ץ נָתַ֥ן לִבְנֵי־אָדָֽם׃ וְ֝הָאָ֗ר Ha-shamáyim shamáyim l-Adonai, v-ha-áretz natan li-vnei adam. exactText: The dead cannot praise YAH, nor those gone down into the dark. וַאֲנַ֤חְנוּ ׀ נְבָ֘רֵ֤ךְ יָ֗ ֥ה וְעַד־עוֹלָ֗ם הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ תָּ מֵעַ Va-anáḥnu nəvarekh Yah me-ˤata və-ˤad ˤolam! Háləlu Yah! But as for us, we bless YAH, now until forever. Praise YAH!70 ָ֑ה וֹמַ֥ע ׀ יְה שְֶׁ י־יִכְִּ י תִּ אָ֭הַבְ חֲנוּנָֽי׃ תַּ ת־ק֝וֹלִ֗י אI love that the CAUSE heard my voice, my petition. ֣ה אׇזְנ֣וֹ לִ֑י טָּ ֽי־הִ כְִּ קְרָֽא׃ וּבְיָמַ֥י א For THEY turned THEIR ear to me, and in my days I call. ת בְלֵי־מָ֗ו ׀ ח9אֲפָפ֤וּנִי א֣וֹל מְצָא֑וּנִי שְֶׁ וּמְצָרֵ֣י The cords of death surround me, the straits of the underworld found me, I found stress and sorrow. מְצָֽא׃ צָרָ֖ה וְיָג֣וֹן א קְרָ֑א ָ֥ה א וֹם־יְה שֵֶֽׁ וּבְ י׃ שִֶֽׁ טָ֥ה נַפְ לְּ ָ֗ה מַ וֹ֥ה יְ֝ה נֶָּ אָ But in the CAUSE’s name I call: please, CAUSE, rescue my soul! ֑יק דִֵּ ָ֣ה וְצַ וֹ֣וּן יְה נֶּ חַ וֵ֖אלֹהֵ֣ינוּ מְרַחֵֽם׃ Gracious is the CAUSE and righteous, and our GOD is compassionate. 69 Psalm 115:1–11 70 Psalm 115:12–18 מג

exactText: ָ֑ה וֹתָאיִ֣ם יְה פְֶּ מֵ֣ר שֶֹׁיעַ׃ שִֶֽׁ ֝לֹּתִ֗י וְלִ֣י יְהוֹ דֵַּ A guardian of the simple is the CAUSE; I was low and THEY would save me. י לִמְנוּחָ֑יְכִי שִֶׁ וּבִ֣י נַ֭פְ שֶׁמַ֥ל עָלָֽיְכִי׃ גָָּ ָ֗ה וֹֽי־יְ֝ה כְִּ Return, my soul, to you resting-place, for the CAUSE rewarded me. ת ֥ו מֵָּ ֗י מִ֫ שִֶׁ נַפְ תָּ ֥צְ לַּ ֤י חִ כְִּ For You drew out my soul from death, my eye from tears, my food from slipping. מְעָ֑ה דִֵּ ת־עֵינִ֥י מִן־ אֽחִי׃ דֵּ ת־רַגְלִ֥י מִ א ָ֑ה וֹךְ לִפְנֵ֣י יְה לֵּ ֭תְהַ אֽים׃ יִּ ֝אַרְצ֗וֹת הַחַ בְּ I would walk before the CAUSE in the land of the living. ֑ר בֵּ ֣י אֲדַ כְִּ י תִּ ֭אֱמַנְ האֲ֝נִ֗י עָנִ֥יתִי מְאֹֽד׃ I believed, as I would speak, I was very afflicted. י בְחׇפְזִ֑י תִּ אֲ֭נִי אָמַ֣רְ זֵֽב׃

exactText: כְֹּֽל־הָאָדָ֥ם כְׇּ I said in haste, every person is a liar.71 ָ֑ה וֹ֥יב לַיה שִֶׁ מָה־אָ גְמוּל֥וֹהִי עָלָֽי׃ תַּ ֽל־ כְׇּ Ma ashiv l-Adonai kol tagmulóhi ˤalai? How can I repay the CAUSE all that has been graced upon me? ֑א שְָּׂ וּע֥וֹת א שֶׁוֹס־יְכְּ קְרָֽא׃ ָ֣ה א וֹ֖ם יְה שֵֶׁ וּבְ Kos yəshuˤot esa uv-shem Adonai eqra. A rescue cup I raise, and in the CAUSE's name I call. ֑ם לֵּ שֶַׁ ָ֣ה אֲ וֹנְ֭דָרַי לַיה ֽוֹ׃ מֵּ ֗֝א לְכׇל־עַ נֶָּ גְדָה־ נ Nədarai l-Adonai ashalem négda na l-khol ˤammo. My vows to the CAUSE I will fulfill, before — please — all THEIR people. ָ֑ה וֹעֵינֵ֣י יְה בְּ יָ֭קָר ֗וְתָה לַחֲסִידָֽיו׃ מֵָּ הַ֝ Yaqar b-ˤeinei Adonai ha-mávta la-ḥasidav. Grevious in the eyes of the CAUSE is the dying of the pious.

exactText: וֹיִ֑ם גָּל־ כְׇּ ָה וֹת־יְ֭ה  הַֽלְל֣וּ א ֽים׃ מִֵּ ל־הָאֻ כְׇּ ח֗וּהוּ בְּ ֝שֶַׁ Háləlu et Adonai kol goyim shabḥúhu kol ha-ummim! Praise the CAUSE, all nations; laud THEM, all peoples! ֗דֵּ ׀ חַסְ 9֥י גָ֘בַ֤ר עָלֵ֨ינ כְִּ ָ֥ה לְעוֹלָ֗ם וֹת־יְה אֱמ ו הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ Ki gavar ˤaléinu ḥasdo ve-emet Adonai l-ˤolam. Háləlu Yah! For great is THEIR kindness over us, and the truth of the CAUSE is forever. Praise Yah!73 For each of the following four lines, everyone at the table responds to each line by repeating the first line. י־ט֑וֹב כְִּ ָ֣ה וֹהוֹד֣וּ לַיה ֽוֹ׃ דֵּ ֖י לְעוֹלָ֣ם חַסְ כְִּ Hodu l-Adonai ki tov, ki l-ˤolam ḥasdo! (Hodu l-Adonai ki tov, ki l- ˤolam ḥasdo!) Thank the CAUSE who is good, for eternal is THEIR kindness! (Thank…) רָאֵ֑ל שְָֽׂ יֹאמַר־נָ֥א יִ ֽוֹ׃ דֵּ ֖י לְעוֹלָ֣ם חַסְ כְִּ Yomar na Yisrael, ki l-ˤolam ḥasdo!

(Hodu l-Adonai ki tov, ki l- ˤolam ḥasdo!) Say, please, God-fearers, for eternal is THEIR kindness! (Thank…)74 ֑ יָּ צַר קָרָ֣אתִי מֵֵּ מִֽן־הַ֭ רְחָ֣ב יָֽהּ׃ מֵּ עָנָ֖נִי בַ Min ha-metzar qaráti Yah; ˤanani va-merḥav Yah. From the straits I called YAH, who answered me with opening. ָ֣ה לִ֭י לֹ֣א אִירָ֑א וֹיְה֖ה לִ֣י אָדָֽם׃ שָֽׂ עֲ יַּמַה־ The CAUSE is with me, I have no fear, what can a mortal do to me?

exactText: עֹזְרָ֑י בְּ ָ֣ה לִ֭י וֹיְהנְאָֽי׃ שָֹֽׂ ֥ה בְ רְא וַ֝אֲנִ֗י א The CAUSE is with me, to help me — and I will see it — against my enemies. ָ֑ה וֹיה בַּ ט֗וֹב לַחֲס֥וֹת אָדָֽם׃ בָּ טֹ֗חַ בְּ מִ֝ It is better to shelter in the CAUSE than trusting in mortals. ָ֑ה וֹיה בַּ ט֗וֹב לַחֲס֥וֹת נְדִיבִֽים׃ בִּ טֹ֗חַ בְּ מִ֝ It is better to shelter in the CAUSE than trusting in princes. וֹיִ֥ם סְבָב֑וּנִי גָּל־ כְׇּ ֣י אֲמִילַֽם׃ כְִּ ָ֗ה וֹ֥ם יְ֝ה שֵֶׁ בְּ All the nations surround me; in the CAUSE's name I cut them down. סַבּ֥וּנִי גַם־סְבָב֑וּנִי ֣י אֲמִילַֽם׃ כְִּ ָ֗ה וֹ֥ם יְ֝ה שֵֶׁ בְּ They surround, yes, all around me; in the CAUSE's name I cut them down. סַבּ֤וּנִי כִדְבוֹרִ֗ים קוֹצִ֑ים שֶׁאֵ֣ כְְּ ֭עֲכוּ דֵֹּThey surround me like bees, flaming up, like fire to thorns; in the CAUSE's name I cut them down. ֣י אֲמִילַֽם׃ כְִּ ָ֗ה וֹ֥ם יְ֝ה שֵֶׁ בְּ 73 Psalm 117 74 Psalm 118:1–4 מה

exactText: ֗ו מֵָּ וְ֝לַ YAH turned me away, turned away, but did not give me to death. ק ֑ד עֲרֵי־צ שֶַׁ תְחוּ־לִ֥י פִֶּ ֥ה יָֽהּ׃ אָבֹא־בָ֗֝ם אוֹד Pitḥu li shaˤarei tsedeq; avo vam, odeh Yah. Open for me the gates of the righteous; I will go in them and thank YAH! ָ֑ה וֹ֥עַר לַיה שַָּׁ ה־הַ ז יקִ֗ים יָבֹ֥אוּ בֽוֹ׃ דִֵּ צַ֝ Zeh ha-sháˤar l-Adonai; tzaddiqim yavó’u vo. This is the gate of the CAUSE; the righteous may go into it!75 From here until the end of the psalm, all of the following lines are repeated. ֣י עֲנִיתָ֑נִי כְִּ א֭וֹדְךָ וּעָֽה׃ שֶׁהִי־לִ֗֝י לִי תְּ וַ Odəkha ki ˤanitáni va-təhi li li-yshuˤa. (Odəkha…) I thank You, for I was low, and You were my salvation. (I thank…) ן מָאֲס֣וּ הַבּוֹנִ֑ים ֭ב א ֽה׃ נֶָּ פִֶּ שֶׁהָ֝יְתָ֗ה לְרֹ֣א Éven ma’asu ha-bonim hayta l-rosh pinna.

exactText: (Éven…) The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone! (The stone…) ֑אתזֶֹּ ָה הָ֣יְתָה וֹמֵאֵ֣ת יְ֭ה עֵינֵֽינוּ׃ בְּ הִ֖יא נִפְלָ֣את Me-et Adonai háyta zot; hi niflat b-ˤeineinu. (Me-et…) From the CAUSE this is; it is wondrous in our eyes! (From the CAUSE…) ָ֑ה וֹ֣ה יְה שָָֽׂ ה־הַ֭יּוֹם עָ ז מְחָ֣ה בֽוֹ׃ שְָֽׂ נָגִ֖ילָה וְנִ Zeh ha-yom ˤasa Adonai; nagíla v-nisməḥa vo. (Zeh…) This day the CAUSE made; let us cheer and rejoice on it. (This day…)76 ָה וֹ֣א יְ֭ה נֶָּ אָ ֑א נֶָּ ֘יעָ֥ה שִֶׁ הוֹAnna Adonai, hoshiˤa na! (Anna Adonai, hoshiˤa na!) Please, CAUSE, please save us! (Please…) ָה וֹ֣א יְ֭ה נֶָּ אָ ֑א נֶָּ ֘יעָ֥ה שִֶׁ הוֹAnna Adonai, hoshiˤa na! (Anna Adonai, hoshiˤa na!) Please, CAUSE, please save us! (Please…) ָ֗ה וֹ֥א יְ֝ה נֶָּ אָ ֽא׃ נֶָּ הַצְלִ֘יחָ֥ה Anna Adonai, hatzliḥa na! (Anna Adonai, hatzliḥa na!) Please, CAUSE, please help us succeed! (Please…) ָ֗ה וֹ֥א יְ֝ה נֶָּ אָ ֽא׃ נֶָּ הַצְלִ֘יחָ֥ה Anna Adonai, hatzliḥa na! (Anna Adonai, hatzliḥa na!) Please, CAUSE, please help us succeed! (Please…)77 75 Psalm 118:5–20 76 Psalm 118:21–24 77 Psalm118: 25 מו

exactText: ָ֑ה וֹ֣ם יְה שֵֶׁ בְּ א בָּ ר֣וּךְ הַ֭ בָּ ָֽה׃ וֹ֥ית יְה בֵּ ֗ם מִ ֝רַ֥כְנוּכ בֵּ Barukh ha-ba b-shem Adonai; berakhnukhem mi-beit Adonai. (Barukh…) Bless who comes in the CAUSE's name; we will bless you from the CAUSE's house. (Bless…) ֫ר לָ֥נ ֢א יָּ ָה֮ וַ וֹאֵ֤ל ׀ יְה עֲבֹתִ֑ים בַּ אִסְרוּ־חַ֥ג ֽחַ׃ בֵּ זְ מִֵּ עַד־קַ֝רְנ֗וֹת הַ El Adonai va-yáer lánu; isru ḥag ba-ˤavotim ˤad qarnot ha-mizbéaḥ. (El Adonai…) God is the CAUSE, and shines on us; bind the festival with cords to the altar’s corners. (God is…) ךָּ ֑ ֣ה וְאוֹד תָּ אֵלִ֣י אַ ׃ ךָּ ֽ אֱ֝לֹהַ֗י אֲרוֹמְמ Eli atta v-odéka, Elohai aroməmeka. (Eli atta…) You are my God and I thank You; my One God, I exult you. (You are…) י־ט֑וֹב כְִּ ָ֣ה וֹהוֹד֣וּ לַיה ֽוֹ׃ דֵּ ֖י לְעוֹלָ֣ם חַסְ כְִּ Hodu l-Adonai ki tov, ki l-ˤolam ḥasdo!

exactText: (Hodu…) Thank the CAUSE who is good, for eternal is THEIR kindness! (Thank…)78 ֽי ֽיךָ וַחֲסִיד שָֽׂ ׇל־מַעֲ כְּ ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ וֹיְהַלְלוּךָ יְה רָאֵל שְָֽׂ ית יִ בֵּ ךָ מְֵּ ךָ וְכׇל־עַ ֽ י רְצוֹנ שֵָֽׂ יקִים עוֹ דִֵּ צַ חוּ וִיפָאֲרוּ וִירוֹמְמוּ בְּ שֶַׁ ה יוֹדוּ וִיבָרְכוּ וִי נֶָּ רִ בְּ י כְִּ ֽנוּ כְֵּ מְךָ מַלְ שִֶׁ ת־ וּ וְיַמְלִֽיכוּ א שֶֽׁי דִֵּ וְיַעֲרִֽיצוּ וְיַקְ י מֵעוֹלָם כְִּ ר מֵֵּ ה לְזַ מְךָ נָא שִֶׁ לְךָ טוֹב לְהוֹדוֹת וּלְ וְעַד עוֹלָם אַתָּה אֵל׃ Let all Your works, CAUSE our God, praise You, and Your pious ones, the righteous who do Your will, and all Your people the house of Israel in cheer will thank and bless and praise and glorify and exult and acclaim and sanctify and crown Your name, our Sovereign, for to You it is good to thank, and to Your name pleasant to hymn, for from eternity until eternity are You.

exactText: Now that we have finished reciting Hallel, we recite Hallel! — the Great Hallel that is, Psalm 136. I have a custom to precede Psalm 136 with Psalm 135, using a reconstructed call and response. Up until naḥala l-Yisrael ˤammo , the response is haləlu Yah. From Adonai shimkha l-ˤolam up to barukh Adonai mi-Tziyon, the response is barəkhu et Adonai. ׀9הַ֥לְלוּ־יָ֨ הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ Praise YAH! (háləlu Yah!) ָ֑ה וֹ֣ם יְה שֵֶׁ ת־ הַֽ֭לְלוּ א הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ Praise the name of the CAUSE! (háləlu Yah!) ָֽה׃ וֹהַֽ֝לְל֗וּ עַבְדֵ֥י יְה הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ Praise, servants of the CAUSE, (háləlu Yah!) ָ֑ה וֹבֵ֣ית יְה בְּ ֣֭עֹמְדִים שֶׁ הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ who stand in the CAUSE’s house; (háləlu Yah!) ֣ית אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ׃ בֵּ ֝חַצְר֗וֹת בְּ הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ in the courts of our God’s house! (háləlu Yah!) ָ֑ה וֹֽי־ט֣וֹב יְה כְִּ הַֽלְלוּ־יָ֭הּ הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ Praise YAH, for the CAUSE is good; (háləlu Yah!) 78 Psalm 118:26–29 מז

exactText: ֣י נָעִֽים׃ כְִּ מ֗וֹ שְֶׁ ר֥וּ לִ֝ מְֵּ זַ הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ Hymn THEIR name for it is sweet! (háləlu Yah!) חַ֣ר ל֣וֹ יָ֑ בָּ ֽי־יַעֲקֹ֗ב כְִּ הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ For YAH chose Yaˤaqov for THEMself; (háləlu Yah!) תֽוֹ׃ לָּ רָאֵ֗ל לִסְגֻ שְָֽׂ יִ֝הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ Israel THEIR treasure. (háləlu Yah!) ָ֑ה וֹֽי־גָד֣וֹל יְה כְִּ י תִּ ֤י אֲנִ֣י יָ֭דַעְ כְִּ הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ For I know the CAUSE is great, (háləlu Yah!) ל־אֱלֹהִֽים׃ כְׇּ וַ֝אֲדֹנֵ֗ינוּ מִ הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ and our Master over all deities. (háləlu Yah!) ֥ה שָָֽׂ ָ֗ה עָ֫ וֹר־חָפֵ֥ץ יְה שֶׁ ֤ל אֲ כְֹּהַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ All that the CAUSE desires is done, (háləlu Yah!) ץ מַ֥יִם וּבָאָ֑ר שָָּׁ בַּ הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ on heaven and on earth, (háləlu Yah!) הֹמֽוֹת׃ תְּ ֗ים וְכׇל־ מִֵּ יַּ֝בַּ הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ in the seas and all the abysses. (háləlu Yah!) ץ אִים֮ מִקְצֵ֢ה הָ֫אָ֥ר שִָֽׂ ֣ה נְ מַעֲל הַֽלְלוּ־יָֽהּ׃ Raising clouds from the earth’s edge, (háləlu Yah!)

(háləlu Yah!) מְךָ֣ לְעוֹלָ֑ם שִֶׁ ָה וֹיְ֭הָֽה׃ וֹת־יְה רְכ֥וּ א בָּ CAUSE, Your name is forever! exactText: We now recite the prayer Nishmat Kol Ḥai, Three fun facts about Nishmat Kol Ḥai: • Although we nowadays associate it with morning services on Shabbat and festivals, it might have originally been said at this time! The Mishnah says that after the great Hallel we recite birkat ha-shir , which Rabbi Yoḥanan says refers to Nishmat Kol Ḥai. This might explain its emphasis on the exodus from Egypt! • A popular legend in medieval Jewish communities states that Nishmat Kol Ḥai was written by none other than St. Peter himself, Shimˤon Kepha. According to rabbinic legend, he remained a pious Jew until the end, and was a deep cover agent for the rabbis in Rome. In any case, the text does have a reverse acrostic of the name Shimˤon.

exactText: • On festivals, modern Ashkenazi practice is to use a special melody at “God, in the might of Your strength” — aka Ha-El. However this has not always been the case. In medieval manuscripts, many communities wrote that they would begin with Ha-El only on Shavuˤot. On Sukkot they would begin with Ha-Gadol, and on Pesaḥ with Ha-Gibor. In honor of this, we’ll use the special melody there — at “Mighty, forever!” נִ ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ וֹמְךָ יְה שִֶׁ ת־ בָרֵךְ א תְּ ׇל־חַי כְּמַת שְֶׁ ֽנוּ כְֵּ פָאֵר וּתְרוֹמֵם זִכְרְךָ מַלְ תְּ ר שָָֽׂ בָּ ׇל־ כְּוְרוּחַ ה אֵל תָּ מִיד מִן־הָעוֹלָם וְעַד־הָעוֹלָם אַ תָּ וֹאֵל וּמוֹשִֽיעַ גָּ ךְ ֽל ֽיךָ אֵין־לָֽנוּ מ לְעָד בַּ וּמִ כׇל־עֵת בְּ יל וּמְפַרְנֵס וּמְרַחֵם צִָּ ה וּמַ וֹדפֶּ ה׃

You manage Your world in kindness and Your creatures in compassion. The CAUSE does not slumber nor sleep! THEY awaken the sleeping, rouse the exhausted, give speech to the silenced, free the bound, support the fallen, straighten the bent! exactText: Please, CAUSE, do not ever abandon us! And thus, the limbs that You set upon us, and the spirit and soul that You breathed within us, and the tongue that You placed in our mouths, will all praise and bless and laud and glorify and exult and exalt and sanctify and acclaim Your name, our Sovereign. ה פֶּ י כׇל־ כְִּ לְךָ ה יוֹדFor every mouth will thank You, וֹןשֶׁ וְכׇל־לָ לְךָ בַע שָָּׁ תִּ every tongue will swear by You, ךְ ֽר בּ וְכׇל־ לְךָ תִכְרַע every eye will look to You, וְכׇל־קוֹמָה ֽיךָ לְפָנ ה חֲו תַּ שְֶׁ תִ every knee will bow before You, וְכׇל־לְבָבוֹת יִירָאוּךָ every heart will revere You, ב וּכְלָיּוֹת ֽר וְכׇל־ק ֽךָ ְמ שֶׁ ְרוּ לִ מֵּ יְזַ every gut will hymn Your Name, אמַרְנָה֮ תֹּ ָ֥ל־עַצְמוֹתַ֨י כְּ תוּב — כְָּ שֶׁ בָר דֵָּ כְַּ וּ וְעָנִ֥י נֶּ ֑ מֵּ ֣יל עָ֭נִי מֵחָזָ֣ק מִ צִָּ ָ֗ה מִ֥י כָ֫מ֥וֹךָ מַ וֹיְה ֽזְלֽוֹ׃

exactText: And it is written: Cheer, you righteous, for the CAUSE; for the upright praise is fitting!83 Sepharadi and egalitarian Traditional Ashkenazi* פִי בְּ רִים שֶָׁ יְׄתְרׄוֹמָם תִּ ל לָּ תְהַ תִּ In the mouths of the upright (yəsharim) You are exalted (titromam)! וּבְדִבְרֵי יקִים דִֵּ צַׄ ׄרַךְ בָּ תְ תִּ רַךְ בָּ תְ תִּ And in the words of the righteous (tzaddiqim) You are blessed (titbarakh)! וֹןשֶׁ וּבִלְ חֲׄסִידִים שֶׁ דֵָּ תְקַׄ תִּ תְרוֹמָם תִּ And in the speech of the pious (ḥasidim) You are sanctified (titqaddash)! ב ֽר קְוּבים שִֶׁ קְׄדוֹ ל׃ לָּ תְהַׄ תִּ ׃שֶׁ דֵָּ תְקַ תִּ And in the midst of the holy (qədoshim) You are praised (tithallal)!

exactText: ךְ ֽל ֽנוּ הָאֵל הַמ כְֵּ מְךָ לָעַד מַלְ שִֶׁ ח בַּ תַּ שְֶׁ יִ ה י־לְךָ נָא כְִּ ץ מַֽיִם וּבָאָֽר שָָּׁ בַּ שֶׁדוֹ קָָּ דוֹל וְהַ גָָּ הַ בָחָה שְֶׁ יר וּ שִֶׁ ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ וֵאלֹהֵי אֲבוֹתֵֽינוּ וֹיְה May Your name, our Sovereign, be lauded forever! God, great and holy Sovereign of heaven and earth. For these are appropriate to You, CAUSE our God and God of our ancestors: power and rule, eternity, greatness and might, 82 Psalm 113:1 83 Psalm 33:1 * Note: the Sepharadic rite, and many Ashkenazim on the Yamim Noraim, arrange the four final verses here to spell out RiVQaH, as a pair to YiTzḤaQ in the nouns. The traditional Ashkenazi practice has these four verbs in a different order — praised, blessed, exalted, sanctified. I prefer the Sepharadic rite for egalitarian reasons. נג

exactText: Please have compassion, CAUSE our God, on Israel Your people and on Jerusalem Your city and on Zion the dwelling-place of Your glory and on Your altar and on Your temple, and rebuild Jerusalem the holy city speedily in our days and bring us up to it to rejoce in its building and eat from its fruit and be sated of its goodness and bless You on it in holiness and purity, [on Friday night add: and desire and strengthen us on this Sabbath day] and make us rejoice on this day of the pilgrimage of matza, for You the CAUSE are good and do good to all... For wine not from Israel:For wine from Israel: ץ ךָ עַל־הָאָֽר לְּ ה־ וְנוֹד ן׃ ָֽפ גָּ רִי הַ פְֶּ וְעַל־ and we thank You for the land and for the fruit of the vine. ץ ךָ עַל־הָאָֽר לְּ ה־ וְנוֹד רִי גַפְנָהּ׃ פְֶּ וְעַל־ and we thank You for the land and for the fruit of its vine. ָה וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ רִי פְֶּ ץ וְעַל־ עַל־הָאָֽר ן׃ ָֽפ גָּ הָ Blessed are You, CAUSE, for the land and for the fruit of the vine. ָה וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ רִי פְֶּ ץ וְעַל־ עַל־הָאָֽר גַפְנָהּ׃ Blessed are You, CAUSE, for the land and for the fruit of its vine. נד

exactText: נִרְצָה AcceptableNirtzah Pray that God accepts this good-willed. With us may Jerusalem once more be filled! Prega Iddio che il bon voler’ accetta e che in Gieruscialaim ce rimetta! ְרֵיגַה אִידִיוֹ קֵי אִיל בּוֹן ווֹלֵיר אַצֵיטַה פֶּ ָלִַם צִי רִימֵטַה׃ שֶׁאֵי קֵי אִין יְרוּ From the Venice Haggadah of 1609 To get us into the mood of Nirtzah, let’s start by reading a (slightly edited) passage from Martin Buber’s Tales of the Ḥasidim: In Russia in the early nineteenth century, it was common to draft Jewish boys into the army, in which they were forced to serve to their sixtieth year. They were known as cantonists. On the eve of Passover, a man whose uniform identified him as a cantonist arrived in Koznitz and asked to be admitted to the holy maggid.

exactText: When he stood in his presence, he begged to be allowed to participate in the Seder, and the maggid gave his permission. When, in the course of the rites of the Seder, they came to the words: “The order of Pesaḥ is concluded,” the guest asked whether he might sing, and his request was granted.After the closing words of the song: “…pəduyim l-Tziyon b-rina,” which means, “redeemed, to Zion in joy,” he cried out in Russian: “Подъём! Podyom!” that is, “Let’s go!” The maggid rose and said in a voice filled with jubilation: “We are ready to go to Zion.” But the guest had vanished. This concluding piyyut fragment, part of a larger cycle by the mid-eleventh century R. Yosef Bonfils, was originally written as a coda to the extensive poetic and halakhic additions for Shabbat haGadol, and first introduced into the seder in the mid-fourteenth century.

exactText: הִלְכָת כְְּ ֽסַח פֶּ וּר דֵּ חֲׄסַל סִ The order of Pesaḥ is concluded, טוֹ וְחֻקָת פֶָּ שְֶׁ כׇל־מִ כְְּ in all its laws, judgements, and rules. ר אוֹת דֵֵּ ר זָכִֽינוּ לְסַ שֶׁ אֲ כְַּ As we merited to order it, וֹתוֹ׃שָֽׂ ה לַעֲ כְּ ן נִזְ כְֵּ so too may we merit to do it! וֹכֵן מְעוֹנָה שֶׁזַׄךְ Pure One, dwelling in the Habitation, קׄוֹמֵם קְהַל עֲדַת מִי־מָנָה raise the congregation of the uncountable community. ה נֶָּ קָרוֹב נַהֵל נִטְעֵי כַ בְּ Quick, gather the saplings planted, ה׃ נֶָּ רִ בְּ דוּיִם לְצִיּוֹן פְֶּ redeemed, to Zion in joy! נוּיָה׃ בְּ ירוּשָלַֽיִם — הַ בִּ אָה בָּ נָה הַ שֶָׁ לְ L-shana ha-ba’a bi-Yrushaláyim — ha-bnuyá! Next year in Jerusalem — rebuilt! נה

exactText: Another piyyut originally written for Shabbat ha-Gadol, the next poem is attributed to Rav Yannai, one of the greatest paytanim of all time. It is recited on the first night in Diaspora communities. We’ll go around and sing or read each stanza in either English or Hebrew. וּבְכֵן ֗יְלָה לַּ חֲצִ֣י הַ בַּ — וַיְהִ֣י ׀ —And so… it was in the middle of the night. אָ בַּ ים הִפְלֵֽאתָ סִִּ ז רוֹב נִ ֽיְלָה לַּ night.Most wondrous miracles You did at בְּ ה הַ ת ז מֽוֹר שְֶׁ אַ שֶׁרֹא ֽיְלָה לַּ night.At first watch, this very גֵ חֱלַק לוֹ נ כְְּ חְתּוֹ צַָּ ק נִ ֽד ר צ לַֽיְלָה night.84 You made the righteous convert victorious when it was divided for him at ֗יְלָה לַּ חֲצִ֣י הַ בַּ וַיְהִ֣י ׀ And it was in the middle of the night.

exactText: יָֽ בַּ פְגָרָיו תָּ שְֶׁ עַץ מְחָרֵף לְנוֹפֵף אִוּוּי הוֹבַֽ ֽיְלָה לַּ night.89 The blasphemer planned to wave the desired; You dressed him in corpses at ָכְּוֹן שֶׁאִי בְּ ל וּמַצָבוֹ בֵּ רַע לַֽיְלָה night.Bel and his pedestal were bent in pitch לְ חֲמוּדוֹת נִגְלָה רָז חֲזוֹת שֶׁאִי לַֽיְלָה night.90The delightful man was shown secret visions of ֗יְלָה לַּ חֲצִ֣י הַ בַּ וַיְהִ֣י ׀ And it was in the middle of the night. מִ בַּ הֱרַג בּוֹ  נ שֶׁ כְלֵי קֹֽד בִּ ר כְֵּ תַּ שְֶׁ ֽיְלָה לַּ night.91The drinker from holy vessels was killed by נעֲתוּתֵי בִּ וֹתֵר פֶּע מִבּוֹר אֲרָיוֹת שֶַׁ וֹלַֽיְלָה night.92The one saved from the lions’s den explained the mares-of- ִשָֽׂבַּ נְאָה נָטַר אֲגָגִי וְכָתַב סְפָרִים ֽיְלָה לַּ night.The Agagite93 kept hatred and wrote books by 84 Abraham. See Genesis 15 and the “covenant between the pieces.” 85 Abimelekh. See Genesis 20:3 86 Laban. See Genesis 31:24 87 See Genesis 32:25–31 88 Sisera. See Judges 5:20. 89 Sennacherib. See Kings 2:19:35. 90 Referring to Daniel. See Daniel 7 for his night vision, and Daniel 10 for his moniker “delightful man.” 91 Belshazzar — see Daniel 5:30 92 Daniel again. The lion’s den is in Daniel 6. He interprets nightmares in chapter 2. 93 Haman. See Esther 3:1 נו

exactText: Diaspora Addition 1! This piyyut, by the great Elazar biRabbi Qallir, is recited on the second night in place of the prior. Many people in Israel recite both, as I found out when I brought a copy of last year’s version of this haggadah to a friend’s seder and he asked why it wasn’t in there. Oops. וּבְכֵן ֨סַח ה֜וּא פֶּ ֽבַח־ ֡ם ז תּ וַאֲמַרְ And so… and you will say, the sacrifice of Pesaḥ is this.98 אֹֽ ֽיךָ הִפְלֵֽאתָ בַּ בוּרוֹת גְָּ ץ מֽסַח פֶּ PesaḥAscendant strength YOU showed on בְּ אתָ שְֵּׂ ל־מוֹעֲדוֹת נִ כְׇּ שֶׁרֹא ֽסַח פֶּ PesaḥBeginning of all festivals YOU set ִגָּזְרָחִי חֲצוֹת לֵיל לִֽיתָ לְא ֽסַח פֶּ PesaḥGiving the Ezraḥite 99 vision on midnight of ֨סַח פֶּ ֽבַח־ ֡ם ז תּ וַאֲמַרְ And you will say, the sacrifice of Pesaḥ.

exactText: 102 Literally, “the Sodomites” but that words has different implications. 103 The lines from Teth to Lamed refer to the tenth plague. 104 Jericho, according to Joshua 5 and 6. “Mured” is an archaic term for “walled” that is used here because it starts with an M. 105 Gideon’s battle in Judges 7 106 Assyria, whose defeat is mentioned in the Samekh and Ayin (O) verses. See Kings 2:19:35 and Isaiah 10:32. While the phrase “Pul and Lydia” is mentioned in Isaiah 66:19, Pul is associated with Assyria in Kings 2:15:19, while Lydia is in Genesis 10:22. 107 See Daniel 5 108 See Isaiah 21:5 109 Esther. See Esther 4 for the three-day fast. The midrash states these fast days, and Haman’s ultimate defeat, both fell during Pesaḥ. 110 Haman 111 Loss of children and widowhood. See Isaiah 47:9. 112 Edom נח

exactText: Ki Lo Naeh This song is thought to date back to the Ḥasidei Ashkenaz of the twelfth century, and shows influence from the heikhalot literature. I like to use a melody first transcribed by the Christian Hebraist Johann Rittangel in the 16th century. ה י לוֹ נָא כְִּ ה י לוֹ יָא כְִּ אַׄדִיר מְלוּכָה בִּ ׄחוּר בָּ הֲלָכָה כְַּ ׄדוּדָיו גְָּ יֹאמְרוּ לוֹ׃ לְךָ וּלְךָ י לְךָ כְִּ לְךָ לְךָ אַף לְךָ מְלָכָה מֵַּ ָה הַ וֹלְךָ יְה ה י לוֹ נָא כְִּ ה י לוֹ יָא כְִּ ׄגוּל דֵָּ מְלוּכָה בִּ הָׄדוּר הֲלָכָה כְַּ וָׄתִיקָיו יֹאמְרוּ לוֹ׃ לְךָ וּלְךָ י לְךָ כְִּ לְךָ לְךָ אַף לְךָ מְלָכָה מֵַּ ָה הַ וֹלְךָ יְה ה י לוֹ נָא כְִּ ה י לוֹ יָא כְִּ יָׄחִיד מְלוּכָה בִּ יר בִּ ׄכְַּ הֲלָכָה כְַּ לִׄמוּדָיו יֹאמְרוּ לוֹ׃

exactText: לְךָ וּלְךָ י לְךָ כְִּ לְךָ לְךָ אַף לְךָ מְלָכָה מֵַּ ָה הַ וֹלְךָ יְה ה י לוֹ נָא כְִּ ה י לוֹ יָא כְִּ For THEM it is right, for THEM it is due! Noble in rule, chosen in law, THEIR armies say to THEM: Yours and Yours, Yours, yes, Yours, Yours, indeed Yours, Yours, CAUSE, is the rule. For THEM it is right, for THEM it is due! Prominent in rule, splendid in law, THEIR elders say to THEM: Yours… Meriting in rule, robust in law, THEIR dignitaries say to THEM: Yours… Unique in rule, mighty in law, THEIR scholars say to THEM: Yours… Reigning in rule, awesome in law, THEIR encirclers say to THEM: Yours… Humble in rule, freeing in law, THEIR righteous say to THEM: Yours… Holy in rule, compassionate in law, THEIR angels say to THEM: Yours… Strong in rule, supportive in law, THEIR pure ones say to THEM: Yours… נט

exactText: Diaspora Addition 2! On the second night in the diaspora, we count the ’omer here. ר שֶׁ ךְ הָעוֹלָם אֲ ֽל ָה אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ מ וֹה יְה תָּ רוּךְ אַ בָּ נוּ שֶָֽׁ דְֵּ קִ מִצְוֺתָיו בְּ ר ֽנוּ עַל־סְפִירַת הָעֹֽמ וָּ וִצִ Blessed are You, CAUSE our God, Sovereign of Eternity, who sanctified us with THEIR commandments and commanded us about counting the ’omer. ר עֹֽמ בָּ חָד הַיּוֹם יוֹם א Today is the first day of the ’omer! ַנָא חַד יוֹמָא בְּעוּמְרָא דֵּהַאיְ Yemenites say it in Aramaic) Adir Hu We’ll be doing Adir Hu in three languages. exactText: This text has grown to be a beloved part of the Western Ashkenazi rite, to the point where the traditional German Jewish greeting after the Seder is “Bau gut,” or “build well!” Since Western Yiddish phonology is much closer to standard German than it is to YIVO, this text has been transcribed using German orthography. אַׄלמעכטיגר גאָט , אַלמעכטיגר גאָט, ל שירהפֶּנוּן בּױא דײַן טעמ אַלזוֹ שיר אוּנ אַלזוֹ באַלד אין אוּנדזערן טאַגן שירה יאַ שירה נוּן בּױא, נוּן בּױא, ל שירהפֶּנוּן בּױא דײַן טעמ Allmächtiger Gott, Allmächtiger Gott, Nun bau dein’ Tempel schiere, Also schier und also bald, In unseren Tagen schiere, Ja schiere; Nun bau, nun bau, Nun bau dein’ Tempel schiere.

exactText: Next up, the Hebrew, along with an English singable acrostic interpretation written by yours truly! Awesome One, Awesome One, Build your Temple quickly Speedily, speedily, in our days, build it quickly. Build it, God! Build it, God! Build your Temple quickly! יר הוּא דִֵּ יר הוּא, אַ דִֵּ אַׄ קָרוֹב. בְּ יתוֹ בֵּ ה יִבְנ קָרוֹב. בְּ יָמֵינוּ בְּ מְהֵרָה, בִּ מְהֵרָה, בִּ נֵה! בְּ נֵה! אֵל בְּ אֵל קָרוֹב!

exactText: Six, I know it! Six are the Mishnah’s orders. Shnem-ˤasar shivtáyya. Aḥad-ˤasar kokhváyya… Twelve, who knows it? Twelve, I know it! Twelve are the tribes. Eleven are [Joseph’s] stars… ר מִי יוֹדֵֽעַ שָָֽׂ ה עָ שֶָׁ ל שְֶׁ ר אֲנִי יוֹדֵֽעַ! שָָֽׂ ה עָ שֶָׁ ל שְֶׁ א׃יָּֽ דֵַּ ר מִ שָָֽׂ ה עָ שֶָׁ ל שְֶׁ איָּבְטַֽ שִֶׁ ר שָָֽׂ נֵים עָ שְֶׁ איָּוֹכְבַֽ כְּר שָָֽׂ אַחַד עָ רַֽיָא בְּ רָה דִ שָָֽׂ עֲ עָה יַרְחֵי לֵדָה שְֶׁ תִּ מוֹנָה יְמֵי מִילָה שְֶׁ תָא בָּ שֶַׁ בְעָה יְמֵי שִֶׁ נָה שְֶׁ ה סִדְרֵי מִ שָָּׁ שִֶׁ י תוֹרָה שֵֶׁ ְמֵּ ה חֻ שָָּׁ חֲמִ הוֹת מֵָּ ע אִ בַּ אַרְ ה אָבוֹת שֶָׁ ל שְֶׁ רִית בְּ נֵי לֻחוֹת הַ שְֶׁ חָד אֱלֹהֵֽינוּ א ץ׃
